    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "        const seriesData = data[team]\r\n            .map((value, index) =\u003e {\r\n                // Skip null/undefined/NaN values if hideNaValues is true\r\n                if (hideNaValues \u0026\u0026 (value === null || value === undefined || isNaN(value))) {\r\n                    return null; // Mark for filtering - don't include in chart at all\r\n                }\r\n                // Calculate size for valid values\r\n                const size = normalizeCircleSize(value);\r\n                return [index, value, size];\r\n            })\r\n            .filter(item =\u003e item !== null); // Remove null entries (n/a values) - they won't be rendered\r\n        \r\n        // Log sample sizes for debugging (first team only)\r\n        if (idx === 0 \u0026\u0026 seriesData.length \u003e 0) {\r\n            console.log('Sample data points with sizes for first team:', seriesData.slice(0, 5).map(d =\u003e ({\r\n                x: d[0],\r\n                y: d[1],\r\n                size: d[2]\r\n            })));\r\n        }\r\n        \r\n        series.push({\r\n            name: team,\r\n            singleAxisIndex: idx,\r\n            coordinateSystem: 'singleAxis',\r\n            type: 'scatter',\r\n            data: seriesData,\r\n            // Use the third element of data array as symbolSize\r\n            symbolSize: function(dataItem) {\r\n                // dataItem format: [x, y, size]\r\n                return dataItem[2] || minCircleSize;\r\n            },\r\n            itemStyle: {\r\n                color: getTeamColor(team)\r\n            }\r\n        });\r\n    });\r\n    \r\n",
        "detailedLines": [

        "originalContents": "        const seriesData = data[team]\r\n            .map((value, index) =\u003e {\r\n                // Skip null/undefined/NaN values if hideNaValues is true\r\n                if (hideNaValues \u0026\u0026 (value === null || value === undefined || isNaN(value))) {\r\n                    return null; // Mark for filtering - don't include in chart at all\r\n                }\r\n                // Calculate size for valid values\r\n                const size = normalizeCircleSize(value);\r\n                return [index, value, size];\r\n            })\r\n            .filter(item =\u003e item !== null); // Remove null entries (n/a values) - they won't be rendered\r\n        \r\n        // Log sample sizes for debugging (first team only)\r\n        if (idx === 0 \u0026\u0026 seriesData.length \u003e 0) {\r\n            console.log('Sample data points with sizes for first team:', seriesData.slice(0, 5).map(d =\u003e ({\r\n                x: d[0],\r\n                y: d[1],\r\n                size: d[2]\r\n            })));\r\n        }\r\n        \r\n        series.push({\r\n            name: team,\r\n            singleAxisIndex: idx,\r\n            coordinateSystem: 'singleAxis',\r\n            type: 'scatter',\r\n            data: seriesData,\r\n            // Use the third element of data array as symbolSize\r\n            symbolSize: function(dataItem) {\r\n                // dataItem format: [x, y, size]\r\n                return dataItem[2] || minCircleSize;\r\n            },\r\n            itemStyle: {\r\n                color: getTeamColor(team)\r\n            }\r\n        });\r\n    });\r\n    \r\n",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "class ResponseDisplayBlock extends BaseContentBlock {\n\r\n    \r\n    ...\n\r\n    \r\n    /**\r\n     * Render chart using existing ECharts functions\r\n     */\r\n    async renderChart() {\r\n        // Wait for container to be available\r\n        await new Promise(resolve =\u003e setTimeout(resolve, 100));\r\n        \r\n        const container = document.getElementById('responseChart');\r\n        if (!container) {\r\n            console.warn('Chart container not found');\r\n            return;\r\n        }\r\n        \r\n        const data = this.currentResponse.data;\r\n        const chartData = this.prepareChartData(data, this.chartType);\r\n        \r\n        if (!chartData) {\r\n            console.warn('Could not prepare chart data');\r\n            return;\r\n        }\r\n        \r\n        try {\r\n            // Use existing chart functions based on chart type\r\n            switch (this.chartType) {\r\n                case 'line':\r\n                    createLineChart(chartData.data, chartData.order, 'responseChart', 'API Response Data', chartData.labels);\r\n                    break;\r\n                case 'bar':\r\n                    createHorizontalBarChart(chartData.values, 'responseChart', 'API Response Data', chartData.labels);\r\n                    break;\r\n                case 'scatter':\r\n                    if (chartData.isTeamPointsFormat) {\r\n                        // Use multi-axis scatter chart for team points data (like \"Points per Match Day\")\r\n                        createScatterChartMultiAxis(chartData.data, chartData.order, 'responseChart', 'Points per Match Day', chartData.labels);\r\n                    } else {\r\n                        // Use vanilla scatter chart for regular array data\r\n                        createScatterChart_vanilla(chartData.scatterData, 'responseChart', 'API Response Data', chartData.axisLabels);\r\n                    }\r\n                    break;",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "class ResponseDisplayBlock extends BaseContentBlock {\r"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "    constructor() {\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "        super"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 21,
            "text": "    }"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 748,
            "text": "getDataPointCount(data) {\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 751,
            "text": " {\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 752,
            "text": "            return Object.keys(data).length;\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 753,
            "text": "        }\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 754,
            "text": "        return 1;\r"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 755,
            "text": "    }\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 756,
            "text": "    \r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 757,
            "text": "    /**\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 758,
            "text": "     * Render chart using existing ECharts functions\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 759,
            "text": "     */\r"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 760,
            "text": "    async renderChart() {\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 761,
            "text": "        // Wait for container to be available\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 762,
            "text": "        await new Promise(resolve =\u003e setTimeout(resolve, 100));\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 763,
            "text": "        \r"
          },

        "originalContents": "\r\n    \r\n    /**\r\n     * Render chart using existing ECharts functions\r\n     */\r\n    async renderChart() {\r\n        // Wait for container to be available\r\n        await new Promise(resolve =\u003e setTimeout(resolve, 100));\r\n        \r\n        const container = document.getElementById('responseChart');\r\n        if (!container) {\r\n            console.warn('Chart container not found');\r\n            return;\r\n        }\r\n        \r\n        const data = this.currentResponse.data;\r\n        const chartData = this.prepareChartData(data, this.chartType);\r\n        \r\n        if (!chartData) {\r\n            console.warn('Could not prepare chart data');\r\n            return;\r\n        }\r\n        \r\n        try {\r\n            // Use existing chart functions based on chart type\r\n            switch (this.chartType) {\r\n                case 'line':\r\n                    createLineChart(chartData.data, chartData.order, 'responseChart', 'API Response Data', chartData.labels);\r\n                    break;\r\n                case 'bar':\r\n                    createHorizontalBarChart(chartData.values, 'responseChart', 'API Response Data', chartData.labels);\r\n                    break;\r\n                case 'scatter':\r\n                    if (chartData.isTeamPointsFormat) {\r\n                        // Use multi-axis scatter chart for team points data (like \"Points per Match Day\")\r\n                        createScatterChartMultiAxis(chartData.data, chartData.order, 'responseChart', 'Points per Match Day', chartData.labels);\r\n                    } else {\r\n                        // Use vanilla scatter chart for regular array data\r\n                        createScatterChart_vanilla(chartData.scatterData, 'responseChart', 'API Response Data', chartData.axisLabels);\r\n                    }\r\n                    break;",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "column": 1,
            "line": 1019

          "startPosition": {
            "column": 3,
            "line": 5
          }

      },
      "score": 0.335120290517807

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "function renderScatterPlot() {\r\n    // Get current selection\r\n    const season = document.querySelector('input[name=\"season\"]:checked')?.value;\r\n    const league = document.querySelector('input[name=\"league\"]:checked')?.value;\r\n    if (!season || !league) {\r\n        alert('Please select a season and league.');\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n    fetch(&#96;/league/get_team_points_vs_average?season=${encodeURIComponent(season)}\u0026league=${encodeURIComponent(league)}&#96;)\r\n        .then(res =\u003e res.json())\r\n        .then(data =\u003e {\r\n            if (!data || typeof data !== 'object' || Array.isArray(data)) {\r\n                console.error('No valid data for scatter plot:', data);\r\n                return;\r\n            }\r\n            const axis_labels = {x: 'Points', y: 'Average'};\r\n            // Use color palette for each team\r\n            if (typeof createScatterChart_vanilla === 'function') {\r\n                // Patch: inject color into data for each team\r\n                const teams = Object.keys(data);\r\n                const coloredData = {};\r\n                teams.forEach((team, idx) =\u003e {\r\n                    coloredData[team] = data[team]; // Data is array of [x, y]\r\n                    // The color will be set in the chart function using getTeamColor(team, idx)\r\n                });\r\n                createScatterChart_vanilla(coloredData, 'chartTeamPointsVsAverage', 'Points vs. Average', axis_labels);\r\n                // Render legend after every second chart (here, always after this chart)\r\n                renderScatterLegend(teams);\r\n            } else {\r\n                console.error('createScatterChart_vanilla is not defined');\r\n            }\r\n        })\r\n        .catch(err =\u003e console.error('Error fetching scatter plot data:', err));\r\n}\r\n\r\n",

        "originalContents": "function renderScatterPlot() {\r\n    // Get current selection\r\n    const season = document.querySelector('input[name=\"season\"]:checked')?.value;\r\n    const league = document.querySelector('input[name=\"league\"]:checked')?.value;\r\n    if (!season || !league) {\r\n        alert('Please select a season and league.');\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n    fetch(&#96;/league/get_team_points_vs_average?season=${encodeURIComponent(season)}\u0026league=${encodeURIComponent(league)}&#96;)\r\n        .then(res =\u003e res.json())\r\n        .then(data =\u003e {\r\n            if (!data || typeof data !== 'object' || Array.isArray(data)) {\r\n                console.error('No valid data for scatter plot:', data);\r\n                return;\r\n            }\r\n            const axis_labels = {x: 'Points', y: 'Average'};\r\n            // Use color palette for each team\r\n            if (typeof createScatterChart_vanilla === 'function') {\r\n                // Patch: inject color into data for each team\r\n                const teams = Object.keys(data);\r\n                const coloredData = {};\r\n                teams.forEach((team, idx) =\u003e {\r\n                    coloredData[team] = data[team]; // Data is array of [x, y]\r\n                    // The color will be set in the chart function using getTeamColor(team, idx)\r\n                });\r\n                createScatterChart_vanilla(coloredData, 'chartTeamPointsVsAverage', 'Points vs. Average', axis_labels);\r\n                // Render legend after every second chart (here, always after this chart)\r\n                renderScatterLegend(teams);\r\n            } else {\r\n                console.error('createScatterChart_vanilla is not defined');\r\n            }\r\n        })\r\n        .catch(err =\u003e console.error('Error fetching scatter plot data:', err));\r\n}\r\n\r\n",

right after position/team group\n            columns.append(ColumnGroup(\n                title=i18n_service.get_text(\"average\"),\n                columns=[\n                    Column(\n                        title=i18n_service.get_text(\"score\"), \n                        field='avg_score', \n                        width='80px', \n                        align='center',\n                        tooltip=f'{i18n_service.get_text(\"average\")} {i18n_service.get_text(\"score\")} vs. all opponents'\n                    ),\n                    Column(\n                        title=i18n_service.get_text(\"points\"), \n                        field='avg_points', \n                        width='80px', \n                        align='center',\n                        tooltip=f'{i18n_service.get_text(\"average\")} {i18n_service.get_text(\"points\")} vs. all opponents'\n                    )\n                ],\n                header_style={\"fontWeight\": \"bold\"}\n            ))",

_**Agent (composer-1)**_


## How Tooltips Are Generated

Tooltips are generated in the `createScatterChartMultiAxis` function in `charts.html` (lines 644-657). The tooltip formatter shows:

1. **Team/Player name** — from `teams[params.seriesIndex]`
2. **Week label** — from `labels[params.data[0]]` (e.g., "Week 1")
3. **Value label and formatted value** — previously hardcoded as "Punkte: ${value}"

## Changes Made

Made the tooltip label configurable:

1. Added `tooltipValueLabel` option to `createScatterChartMultiAxis` (defaults to "Punkte" for backward compatibility)
2. Updated the formatter to:
   - Use the configurable label
   - Format percentages with 1 decimal place and add "%"
   - Format other values appropriately (integers vs. decimals)
3. Updated both chart calls in `team-performance-block.js`:
   - Average chart: uses "Punkte" (or translated "pins")
   - Win percentage chart: uses "Win %" (or translated equivalent)

Now the tooltips will show:
- Average chart: "Player Name<br/>Week 1<br/>Punkte: 180"
- Win percentage chart: "Player Name<br/>Week 1<br/>Win %: 75.5%"

The tooltip formatter handles null/undefined values by showing "N/A (No participation)" when a player didn't participate in that week.

---

        "contents": "                if (field.startsWith('week_')) {\r\n                    console.log(&#96;[Decimal Debug] Field: \"${field}\", decimalPlaces: ${decimalPlaces}, column.decimal_places: ${column.decimal_places}, column keys:&#96;, Object.keys(column));\r\n                }\r\n                \r\n                const formatter = function(cell) {\r\n                    const value = cell.getValue();\r\n                    const element = cell.getElement();\r\n                    const rowData = cell.getRow().getData();\r\n                    const rowIdx = rowData.__rowIndex;\r\n                    const row = cell.getRow();\r\n\r\n                    // Add highlighting class and style to cell element if group is highlighted\r\n                    if (isHighlighted) {\r\n                        element.classList.add('tab-col-highlighted');\r\n                        element.style.setProperty('font-weight', HIGHLIGHT_STYLES.cellFontWeight, 'important');\r\n                    }\r\n\r\n                    // Column-level inline styles\r\n                    applyElementStyles(element, columnStyles);\r\n\r\n                    // Cell metadata styles (heatmap colors) -         "originalContents": "                if (field.startsWith('week_')) {\r\n                    console.log(&#96;[Decimal Debug] Field: \"${field}\", decimalPlaces: ${decimalPlaces}, column.decimal_places: ${column.decimal_places}, column keys:&#96;, Object.keys(column));\r\n                }\r\n                \r\n                const formatter = function(cell) {\r\n                    const value = cell.getValue();\r\n                    const element = cell.getElement();\r\n                    const rowData = cell.getRow().getData();\r\n                    const rowIdx = rowData.__rowIndex;\r\n                    const row = cell.getRow();\r\n\r\n                    // Add highlighting class and style to cell element if group is highlighted\r\n                    if (isHighlighted) {\r\n                        element.classList.add('tab-col-highlighted');\r\n                        element.style.setProperty('font-weight', HIGHLIGHT_STYLES.cellFontWeight, 'important');\r\n                    }\r\n\r\n                    // Column-level inline styles\r\n                    applyElementStyles(element, columnStyles);\r\n\r\n                    // Cell metadata styles (heatmap colors) - apply with !important to override striping\r\n                    const cellMeta = cellMetadataMap[rowIdx]?.[field];\r\n                    if (cellMeta) {\r\n                        // Apply heatmap background color with !important to override striping CSS\r\n                        if (cellMeta.backgroundColor) {\r\n                            element.style.setProperty('background-color', cellMeta.backgroundColor, 'important');\r\n                        }\r\n                        // Apply other styles normally\r\n                        Object.entries(cellMeta).forEach(([property, value]) =\u003e {\r\n                            if (property !== 'backgroundColor' \u0026\u0026 value !== undefined \u0026\u0026 value !== null) {\r\n                                element.style[property] = value;\r\n                            }\r\n                        });\r\n                    }\r\n",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\r\n\r\n\r\ndef apply_heat_map_to_columns(table_data: List[List], cell_metadata: Dict[str, Dict],\r\n                              column_indices: List[int], min_val: float = None, max_val: float = None) -\u003e Dict[str, Dict]:\r\n    \"\"\"\r\n    Apply heat map coloring to specified column indices.\r\n    \r\n    Args:\r\n        table_data: List of rows, where each row is a list of values\r\n        cell_metadata: Dictionary mapping \"row:col\" to cell metadata\r\n        column_indices: List of column indices (0-based) to apply coloring to\r\n        min_val: Optional minimum value for color scale. If None, calculated from data\r\n        max_val: Optional maximum value for color scale. If None, calculated from data\r\n        \r\n    Returns:\r\n        Updated cell_metadata dictionary\r\n    \"\"\"\r\n    if not table_data or not column_indices:\r\n        return cell_metadata\r\n    \r\n    # Extract all values from specified columns\r\n    all_values = []\r\n    for row in table_data:\r\n        for col_idx in column_indices:\r\n            if col_idx \u003c len(row):\r\n                value = row[col_idx]\r\n                # Only include numeric values (skip empty strings, None, etc.)\r\n                if isinstance(value, (int, float)) and value != \"\":\r\n                    all_values.append(value)\r\n    \r\n    if not all_values:\r\n        return cell_metadata\r\n    \r\n    # Calculate min/max if not provided\r\n    calculated_min = min(all_values) if min_val is None else min_val\r\n    calculated_max = max(all_values) if max_val is None else max_val\r\n    \r\n    # Apply coloring to each cell in specified columns\r\n    for row_idx, row in enumerate(table_data):\r\n        for col_idx in column_indices:\r\n            if col_idx \u003c len(row):\r\n                value = row[col_idx]\r\n                if isinstance(value, (int, float)) and value != \"\":\r\n                    color = get_heat_map_color(value, calculated_min, calculated_max)\r\n                    cell_metadata[f\"{row_idx}:{col_idx}\"] = {\"backgroundColor\": color}\r\n    \r\n    return cell_metadata",

        "originalContents": "\r\n\r\n\r\ndef apply_heat_map_to_columns(table_data: List[List], cell_metadata: Dict[str, Dict],\r\n                              column_indices: List[int], min_val: float = None, max_val: float = None) -\u003e Dict[str, Dict]:\r\n    \"\"\"\r\n    Apply heat map coloring to specified column indices.\r\n    \r\n    Args:\r\n        table_data: List of rows, where each row is a list of values\r\n        cell_metadata: Dictionary mapping \"row:col\" to cell metadata\r\n        column_indices: List of column indices (0-based) to apply coloring to\r\n        min_val: Optional minimum value for color scale. If None, calculated from data\r\n        max_val: Optional maximum value for color scale. If None, calculated from data\r\n        \r\n    Returns:\r\n        Updated cell_metadata dictionary\r\n    \"\"\"\r\n    if not table_data or not column_indices:\r\n        return cell_metadata\r\n    \r\n    # Extract all values from specified columns\r\n    all_values = []\r\n    for row in table_data:\r\n        for col_idx in column_indices:\r\n            if col_idx \u003c len(row):\r\n                value = row[col_idx]\r\n                # Only include numeric values (skip empty strings, None, etc.)\r\n                if isinstance(value, (int, float)) and value != \"\":\r\n                    all_values.append(value)\r\n    \r\n    if not all_values:\r\n        return cell_metadata\r\n    \r\n    # Calculate min/max if not provided\r\n    calculated_min = min(all_values) if min_val is None else min_val\r\n    calculated_max = max(all_values) if max_val is None else max_val\r\n    \r\n    # Apply coloring to each cell in specified columns\r\n    for row_idx, row in enumerate(table_data):\r\n        for col_idx in column_indices:\r\n            if col_idx \u003c len(row):\r\n                value = row[col_idx]\r\n                if isinstance(value, (int, float)) and value != \"\":\r\n                    color = get_heat_map_color(value, calculated_min, calculated_max)\r\n                    cell_metadata[f\"{row_idx}:{col_idx}\"] = {\"backgroundColor\": color}\r\n    \r\n    return cell_metadata",

        "contents": "            data.columns.forEach((colGroup, groupIndex) =\u003e {\r\n                if (colGroup.columns) {\r\n                    colGroup.columns.forEach((col, index) =\u003e {\r\n                        const td = document.createElement('td');\r\n                        td.style.padding = '8px';\r\n                        td.style.textAlign = col.align || 'center';\r\n                        \r\n                        // Get the value for this cell\r\n                        const value = rowData[colIndex];\r\n                        colIndex++;\r\n                        // Apply dynamic heat map colors based on cell value (for sorting compatibility)\r\n                        if (data.metadata \u0026\u0026 data.metadata.score_range \u0026\u0026 data.metadata.points_range) {\r\n                            if (value === \"\") {\r\n                                // Empty cells (diagonal) get light gray background\r\n                                td.style.backgroundColor = ColorUtils.getThemeColor('background') || \"#f8f9fa\";\r\n                            ",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "} else {\r\n                                const cellValue = parseFloat(value);\r\n                                if (!isNaN(cellValue)) {\r\n                                    // Determine if this is a score or points column based on column position\r\n                                    // Skip the first column (team name) - colIndex starts at 1 after team name\r\n                                    const adjustedColIndex = colIndex - 1; // Adjust for team name column\r\n                                    // Based on debug output: Col 4 (adjustedColIndex 3) has score 751, Col 5 (adjustedColIndex 4) has points 5\r\n                                    // So the pattern is: Score (odd adjustedColIndex), Points (even adjustedColIndex)\r\n                                    const isScoreColumn = adjustedColIndex % 2 === 1; // Score columns are odd positions (1, 3, 5, 7...)\r\n                                    const isPointsColumn = adjustedColIndex % 2 === 0; // Points columns are even positions (0, 2, 4, 6...)\r\n                                    \r\n                                    if (isScoreColumn \u0026\u0026 data.metadata.score_range) {\r\n                                        const color = ColorUtils.getHeatMapColor(cellValue, data.metadata.score_range.min, data.metadata.score_range.max);\r\n                                        td.style.backgroundColor = color;\r\n                                    } else if (isPointsColumn \u0026\u0026 data.metadata.points_range) {\r\n                                        const color = ColorUtils.getHeatMapColor(cellValue, data.metadata.points_range.min, data.metadata.points_range.max);\r\n                                        td.style.backgroundColor = color;\r\n                                    }\r\n                                }\r\n                            }\r\n",
        "detailedLines": [

            "lineNumber": 1919,

            "lineNumber": 1920,

            "lineNumber": 1921,
            "text": "                                    // So the pattern is: Score (odd adjustedColIndex), Points (even adjustedColIndex)\r"

        "originalContents": "} else {\r\n                                const cellValue = parseFloat(value);\r\n                                if (!isNaN(cellValue)) {\r\n                                    // Determine if this is a score or points column based on column position\r\n                                    // Skip the first column (team name) - colIndex starts at 1 after team name\r\n                                    const adjustedColIndex = colIndex - 1; // Adjust for team name column\r\n                                    // Based on debug output: Col 4 (adjustedColIndex 3) has score 751, Col 5 (adjustedColIndex 4) has points 5\r\n                                    // So the pattern is: Score (odd adjustedColIndex), Points (even adjustedColIndex)\r\n                                    const isScoreColumn = adjustedColIndex % 2 === 1; // Score columns are odd positions (1, 3, 5, 7...)\r\n                                    const isPointsColumn = adjustedColIndex % 2 === 0; // Points columns are even positions (0, 2, 4, 6...)\r\n                                    \r\n                                    if (isScoreColumn \u0026\u0026 data.metadata.score_range) {\r\n                                        const color = ColorUtils.getHeatMapColor(cellValue, data.metadata.score_range.min, data.metadata.score_range.max);\r\n                                        td.style.backgroundColor = color;\r\n                                    } else if (isPointsColumn \u0026\u0026 data.metadata.points_range) {\r\n                                        const color = ColorUtils.getHeatMapColor(cellValue, data.metadata.points_range.min, data.metadata.points_range.max);\r\n                                        td.style.backgroundColor = color;\r\n                                    }\r\n                                }\r\n                            }\r\n",

        "contents": "class LeagueService:\n    ...\n    def get_team_vs_team_comparison_table(self, league: str, season: str, week: int = None) -\u003e 'TableData':\n\n                ...\n\n                for opponent in sorted_teams:\n                    if team != opponent:\n                        if opponent in comparison_data[team]:\n                            score = comparison_data[team][opponent]['avg_score']\n                            points = comparison_data[team][opponent]['avg_points']\n                        else:\n                            score = 0.0\n                            points = 0.0\n                        \n                        row.extend([score, points])\n                        col_idx += 2\n                    else:\n                        # Diagonal cells - empty cells\n                        row.extend([\"\", \"\"])\n                        cell_metadata[f\"{row_idx}:{col_idx}\"] = {\"backgroundColor\": get_theme_color(\"background\")}\n                        cell_metadata[f\"{row_idx}:{col_idx + 1}\"] = {\"backgroundColor\": get_theme_color(\"background\")}\n                        col_idx += 2\n                \n                table_data.append(row)\n            \n            # ========== APPLY HEAT MAP COLORING ==========\n            # Apply heat map using generic function (automatically skips empty diagonal cells)\n            self._apply_heat_map_to_columns(table_data, cell_metadata, score_column_indices, score_min, score_max)\n            self._apply_heat_map_to_columns(table_data, cell_metadata, points_column_indices, points_min, points_max)\n            self._apply_heat_map_to_columns(table_data, cell_metadata, [avg_score_col_idx], score_min, score_max)\n            self._apply_heat_map_to_columns(table_data, cell_metadata, [avg_points_col_idx], points_min, points_max)",

 // Light table body text\r\n        tableMutedText: \"#94a3b8\",    // Muted text (dark mode)\r\n        \r\n        // Heat map colors (same as light mode)\r\n        heatMapStart: \"#dddddd\",",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\"\"\"\r\nColor constants matching ColorUtils.THEME_COLORS from color-utils.js\r\nThese should be kept in sync with the JavaScript ColorUtils definitions.\r\n\"\"\"\r\n\r\n# Theme colors - matching ColorUtils.THEME_COLORS\r\nTHEME_COLORS = {\r\n    # Primary colors\r\n    \"primary\": \"#1B8CA6\",           # Dark blue - navbar, card headers\r\n    \"secondary\": \"#4aa8c2\",         # Light blue - buttons, interactive elements\r\n    \"accent\": \"#7dcfe6\",             # Lightest blue - highlights, accents\r\n    \r\n    # Background colors\r\n    \"background\": \"#f8f9fa\",        # Main page background\r\n    \"surface\": \"#ffffff\",           # Card/container background\r\n    \"surface_alt\": \"#e9ecef\",       # Alternate surface (lighter gray)\r\n    \"surface_light\": \"#f0f0f0\",     # Light gray variant\r\n    \r\n    # Text colors\r\n    \"text_on_primary\": \"#ffffff\",   # Text on primary background\r\n    \"text_on_secondary\": \"#ffffff\",  # Text on secondary background\r\n    \"text_on_light\": \"#1F77B4\",     # Text on light background\r\n    \r\n    # Border colors\r\n    \"border\": \"#264653\",             # Dark teal/green borders\r\n    \"border_light\": \"#dee2e6\",      # Light borders\r\n    \r\n    # Table colors\r\n    \"table_header_bg\": \"#eef2f7\",   # Table header background\r\n    \"table_header_text\": \"#0f172a\", # Table header text\r\n    \"table_body_text\": \"#1f2933\",   # Table body text\r\n    \"table_muted_text\": \"#64748b\",  # Muted text\r\n    \r\n    # Heat map colors\r\n    \"heat_map_start\": \"#dddddd\",    # Heat map start color\r\n    \"heat_map_end\": \"#1b8da7\",      # Heat map end color\r\n    \"heat_map_low\": \"#d9596a\",      # Heat map low value\r\n    \"heat_map_high\": \"#1b8da7\",     # Heat map high value\r\n    \r\n    # Status colors\r\n    \"warning\": \"#86e1b3\",            # Warning states\r\n    \"info\": \"#a1e8c4\",              # Info states\r\n    \"success\": \"#d4edda\",           # Success states\r\n    \"danger\": \"#f8d7da\",            # Danger states\r\n}",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "\"\"\"\r"

        "originalContents": "\"\"\"\r\nColor constants matching ColorUtils.THEME_COLORS from color-utils.js\r\nThese should be kept in sync with the JavaScript ColorUtils definitions.\r\n\"\"\"\r\n\r\n# Theme colors - matching ColorUtils.THEME_COLORS\r\nTHEME_COLORS = {\r\n    # Primary colors\r\n    \"primary\": \"#1B8CA6\",           # Dark blue - navbar, card headers\r\n    \"secondary\": \"#4aa8c2\",         # Light blue - buttons, interactive elements\r\n    \"accent\": \"#7dcfe6\",             # Lightest blue - highlights, accents\r\n    \r\n    # Background colors\r\n    \"background\": \"#f8f9fa\",        # Main page background\r\n    \"surface\": \"#ffffff\",           # Card/container background\r\n    \"surface_alt\": \"#e9ecef\",       # Alternate surface (lighter gray)\r\n    \"surface_light\": \"#f0f0f0\",     # Light gray variant\r\n    \r\n    # Text colors\r\n    \"text_on_primary\": \"#ffffff\",   # Text on primary background\r\n    \"text_on_secondary\": \"#ffffff\",  # Text on secondary background\r\n    \"text_on_light\": \"#1F77B4\",     # Text on light background\r\n    \r\n    # Border colors\r\n    \"border\": \"#264653\",             # Dark teal/green borders\r\n    \"border_light\": \"#dee2e6\",      # Light borders\r\n    \r\n    # Table colors\r\n    \"table_header_bg\": \"#eef2f7\",   # Table header background\r\n    \"table_header_text\": \"#0f172a\", # Table header text\r\n    \"table_body_text\": \"#1f2933\",   # Table body text\r\n    \"table_muted_text\": \"#64748b\",  # Muted text\r\n    \r\n    # Heat map colors\r\n    \"heat_map_start\": \"#dddddd\",    # Heat map start color\r\n    \"heat_map_end\": \"#1b8da7\",      # Heat map end color\r\n    \"heat_map_low\": \"#d9596a\",      # Heat map low value\r\n    \"heat_map_high\": \"#1b8da7\",     # Heat map high value\r\n    \r\n    # Status colors\r\n    \"warning\": \"#86e1b3\",            # Warning states\r\n    \"info\": \"#a1e8c4\",              # Info states\r\n    \"success\": \"#d4edda\",           # Success states\r\n    \"danger\": \"#f8d7da\",            # Danger states\r\n}",
        "range": {

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "                    if (field === 'player_initials') {\r\n                        const playerName = rowData['player_name'] || null;\r\n                        if (playerName \u0026\u0026 value !== null \u0026\u0026 value !== undefined \u0026\u0026 value !== '') {\r\n                            // Normalize player name (trim whitespace) to ensure it matches the color map\r\n                            const normalizedPlayerName = String(playerName).trim();\r\n                            // Try multiple sources for player color - access directly at render time\r\n                            let color = null;\r\n                            // First try window.ColorUtils.playerColorMap (most reliable)\r\n                            if (window.ColorUtils \u0026\u0026 window.ColorUtils.playerColorMap) {\r\n                                color = window.ColorUtils.playerColorMap[normalizedPlayerName];\r\n                            }\r\n                            // Then try window.playerColorMap\r\n                            if (!color \u0026\u0026 window.playerColorMap) {\r\n                                color = window.playerColorMap[normalizedPlayerName];\r\n                            }\r\n                            // Fallback to getTeamColor which should check playerColorMap\r\n                            if (!color) {\r\n                                color = getTeamColor(normalizedPlayerName);\r\n                            }\r\n                            // Final fallback to a visible color (not gray, use a default palette color)\r\n",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 410,
            "text": "                    // Special handling for player_initials column (colored circle with player initial)\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 411,
            "text": "                    if (field === 'player_initials') {\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 412,
            "text": "                        const playerName = rowData['player_name'] || null;\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 413,
            "text": "                        if (playerName \u0026\u0026 value !== null \u0026\u0026 value !== undefined \u0026\u0026 value !== '') {\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 414,
            "text": "                            // Normalize player name (trim whitespace) to ensure it matches the color map\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 415,
            "text": "                            const normalizedPlayerName = String(playerName).trim();\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 416,
            "text": "                            // Try multiple sources for player color - access directly at render time\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 417,
            "text": "                            let color = null;\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 418,
            "text": "                            // First try window.ColorUtils.playerColorMap (most reliable)\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 419,
            "text": "                            if (window.ColorUtils \u0026\u0026 window.ColorUtils.playerColorMap) {\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 420,
            "text": "                                color = window.ColorUtils.playerColorMap[normalizedPlayerName];\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 421,
            "text": "                            }\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 422,
            "text": "                            // Then try window.playerColorMap\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 423,
            "text": "                            if (!color \u0026\u0026 window.playerColorMap) {\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 424,
            "text": "                                color = window.playerColorMap[normalizedPlayerName];\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 425,
            "text": "                            }\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 426,
            "text": "                            // Fallback to getTeamColor which should check playerColorMap\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 427,
            "text": "                            if (!color) {\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 428,
            "text": "                                color = getTeamColor(normalizedPlayerName);\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 429,
            "text": "                            }\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 430,
            "text": "                            // Final fallback to a visible color (not gray, use a default palette color)\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 431,
            "text": "                            if (!color || color === '#888') {\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 432,
            "text": "                                "
          }
        ],
        "originalContents": "                    if (field === 'player_initials') {\r\n                        const playerName = rowData['player_name'] || null;\r\n                        if (playerName \u0026\u0026 value !== null \u0026\u0026 value !== undefined \u0026\u0026 value !== '') {\r\n                            // Normalize player name (trim whitespace) to ensure it matches the color map\r\n                            const normalizedPlayerName = String(playerName).trim();\r\n                            // Try multiple sources for player color - access directly at render time\r\n                            let color = null;\r\n                            // First try window.ColorUtils.playerColorMap (most reliable)\r\n                            if (window.ColorUtils \u0026\u0026 window.ColorUtils.playerColorMap) {\r\n                                color = window.ColorUtils.playerColorMap[normalizedPlayerName];\r\n                            }\r\n                            // Then try window.playerColorMap\r\n                            if (!color \u0026\u0026 window.playerColorMap) {\r\n                                color = window.playerColorMap[normalizedPlayerName];\r\n                            }\r\n                            // Fallback to getTeamColor which should check playerColorMap\r\n                            if (!color) {\r\n                                color = getTeamColor(normalizedPlayerName);\r\n                            }\r\n                            // Final fallback to a visible color (not gray, use a default palette color)\r\n",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "column": 32,

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\r\n\r\n\r\ndef get_heat_map_color(value: float, min_val: float, max_val: float, \r\n                       start_color: str = None, end_color: str = None,\r\n                       use_low_high: bool = True) -\u003e str:\r\n    \"\"\"\r\n    Generate heat map color interpolating between start and end colors.\r\n    Matches the JavaScript ColorUtils.getHeatMapColor implementation.\r\n    \r\n    Args:\r\n        value: The value to map to a color\r\n        min_val: Minimum value in the range\r\n        max_val: Maximum value in the range\r\n        start_color: Optional start color (low value). If None, uses theme color\r\n        end_color: Optional end color (high value). If None, uses theme color\r\n        use_low_high: If True (default), uses heat_map_low/high. If False, uses heat_map_start/end\r\n        \r\n    Returns:\r\n        RGB color string (e.g., \"rgb(123, 45, 67)\")\r\n    \"\"\"\r\n    if value == \"\" or value is None or min_val == max_val:\r\n        return get_theme_color(\"background\") or \"#f8f9fa\"\r\n    \r\n    # Use theme colors if not specified\r\n    if start_color is None:\r\n        if use_low_high:\r\n            start_color = THEME_COLORS.get(\"heat_map_low\", \"#d9596a\")\r\n        else:\r\n            start_color = THEME_COLORS.get(\"heat_map_start\", \"#dddddd\")\r\n    if end_color is None:\r\n        if use_low_high:\r\n            end_color = THEME_COLORS.get(\"heat_map_high\", \"#1b8da7\")\r\n        else:\r\n            end_color = THEME_COLORS.get(\"heat_map_end\", \"#1b8da7\")\r\n    \r\n    # Normalize value to 0-1 range, clamped between 0 and 1\r\n    ratio = (value - min_val) / (max_val - min_val) if max_val != min_val else 0\r\n    ratio = max(0, min(1, ratio))  # Clamp between 0 and 1\r\n    \r\n    # Convert hex colors to RGB\r\n    r1, g1, b1 = hex_to_rgb(start_color)\r\n    r2, g2, b2 = hex_to_rgb(end_color)\r\n    \r\n    # Interpolate between the two colors\r\n    r = round(r1 + (r2 - r1) * ratio)\r\n    g = round(g1 + (g2 - g1) * ratio)\r\n    b = round(b1 + (b2 - b1) * ratio)\r\n    \r\n    return f\"rgb({r}, {g}, {b})\"",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 61,
            "text": "def hex_to_rgb(hex_color: str) -\u003e tuple:\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 71,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 72,
            "text": "    return tuple(int(hex_color[i:i+2], 16) for i in (0, 2, 4))\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 73,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 74,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 75,
            "text": "def get_heat_map_color(value: float, min_val: float, max_val: float, \r"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 76,
            "text": "                       start_color: str = None, end_color: str = None,\r"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 77,
            "text": "                       use_low_high: bool = True) -\u003e str:\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 78,
            "text": "    \"\"\"\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 79,
            "text": "    Generate heat map color interpolating between start and end colors.\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 80,
            "text": "    Matches the JavaScript ColorUtils.getHeatMapColor implementation.\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 81,
            "text": "    \r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 82,
            "text": "    Args:\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 83,
            "text": "        value: The value to map to a color\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 84,
            "text": "        min_val: Minimum value in the range\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 85,
            "text": "        max_val: Maximum value in the range\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 86,
            "text": "        start_color: Optional start color (low value). If None, uses theme color\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 87,
            "text": "        end_color: Optional end color (high value). If None, uses theme color\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 88,
            "text": "        use_low_high: If True (default), uses heat_map_low/high. If False, uses heat_map_start/end\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 89,
            "text": "        \r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 90,
            "text": "    Returns:\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 91,
            "text": "        RGB color string (e.g., \"rgb(123, 45, 67)\")\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 92,
            "text": "    \"\"\"\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 93,
            "text": "    if value == \"\" or value is None or min_val == max_val:\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 94,
            "text": "        return get_theme_color(\"background\") or \"#f8f9fa\"\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 95,
            "text": "    \r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 96,
            "text": "    # Use theme colors if not specified\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 97,
            "text": "    if start_color is None:\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 98,
            "text": "        if use_low_high:\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 99,
            "text": "            start_color = THEME_COLORS.get(\"heat_map_low\", \"#d9596a\")\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 100,
            "text": "        else:\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 101,
            "text": "            start_color = THEME_COLORS.get(\"heat_map_start\", \"#dddddd\")\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 102,
            "text": "    if end_color is None:\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 103,
            "text": "        if use_low_high:\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 104,
            "text": "            end_color = THEME_COLORS.get(\"heat_map_high\", \"#1b8da7\")\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 105,
            "text": "        else:\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 106,
            "text": "            end_color = THEME_COLORS.get(\"heat_map_end\", \"#1b8da7\")\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 107,
            "text": "    \r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 108,
            "text": "    # Normalize value to 0-1 range, clamped between 0 and 1\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 109,
            "text": "    ratio = (value - min_val) / (max_val - min_val) if max_val != min_val else 0\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 110,
            "text": "    ratio = max(0, min(1, ratio))  # Clamp between 0 and 1\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 111,
            "text": "    \r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 112,
            "text": "    # Convert hex colors to RGB\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 113,
            "text": "    r1, g1, b1 = hex_to_rgb(start_color)\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 114,
            "text": "    r2, g2, b2 = hex_to_rgb(end_color)\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 115,
            "text": "    \r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 116,
            "text": "    # Interpolate between the two colors\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 117,
            "text": "    r = round(r1 + (r2 - r1) * ratio)\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 118,
            "text": "    g = round(g1 + (g2 - g1) * ratio)\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 119,
            "text": "    b = round(b1 + (b2 - b1) * ratio)\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 120,
            "text": "    \r"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 121,
            "text": "    return f\"rgb({r}, {g}, {b})\""
          }
        ],
        "originalContents": "\r\n\r\n\r\ndef get_heat_map_color(value: float, min_val: float, max_val: float, \r\n                       start_color: str = None, end_color: str = None,\r\n                       use_low_high: bool = True) -\u003e str:\r\n    \"\"\"\r\n    Generate heat map color interpolating between start and end colors.\r\n    Matches the JavaScript ColorUtils.getHeatMapColor implementation.\r\n    \r\n    Args:\r\n        value: The value to map to a color\r\n        min_val: Minimum value in the range\r\n        max_val: Maximum value in the range\r\n        start_color: Optional start color (low value). If None, uses theme color\r\n        end_color: Optional end color (high value). If None, uses theme color\r\n        use_low_high: If True (default), uses heat_map_low/high. If False, uses heat_map_start/end\r\n        \r\n    Returns:\r\n        RGB color string (e.g., \"rgb(123, 45, 67)\")\r\n    \"\"\"\r\n    if value == \"\" or value is None or min_val == max_val:\r\n        return get_theme_color(\"background\") or \"#f8f9fa\"\r\n    \r\n    # Use theme colors if not specified\r\n    if start_color is None:\r\n        if use_low_high:\r\n            start_color = THEME_COLORS.get(\"heat_map_low\", \"#d9596a\")\r\n        else:\r\n            start_color = THEME_COLORS.get(\"heat_map_start\", \"#dddddd\")\r\n    if end_color is None:\r\n        if use_low_high:\r\n            end_color = THEME_COLORS.get(\"heat_map_high\", \"#1b8da7\")\r\n        else:\r\n            end_color = THEME_COLORS.get(\"heat_map_end\", \"#1b8da7\")\r\n    \r\n    # Normalize value to 0-1 range, clamped between 0 and 1\r\n    ratio = (value - min_val) / (max_val - min_val) if max_val != min_val else 0\r\n    ratio = max(0, min(1, ratio))  # Clamp between 0 and 1\r\n    \r\n    # Convert hex colors to RGB\r\n    r1, g1, b1 = hex_to_rgb(start_color)\r\n    r2, g2, b2 = hex_to_rgb(end_color)\r\n    \r\n    # Interpolate between the two colors\r\n    r = round(r1 + (r2 - r1) * ratio)\r\n    g = round(g1 + (g2 - g1) * ratio)\r\n    b = round(b1 + (b2 - b1) * ratio)\r\n    \r\n    return f\"rgb({r}, {g}, {b})\"",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "column": 32,
            "line": 120
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 60
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "app/utils/color_constants.py"
      },
      "score": 0.3732976019382477
    },

 // Get actual colspan from the group\r\n        for (let i = 0; i \u003c colspan; i++) {\r\n            columnColors[currentColumn] = colors[groupIndex];\r\n            lastGroupColumns[currentColumn] = isLastGroup;\r\n            currentColumn++;\r\n        }\r\n    });\r\n\r\n    const createHeaderGroups = () =\u003e {\r\n        if (!headerGroups || headerGroups.length === 0) return '';\r\n        \r\n        return &#96;\r\n            \u003ctr\u003e\r\n                ${headerGroups.map((group, index) =\u003e &#96;\r\n                    \u003cth class=\"text-center\" \r\n                        colspan=\"${group.colspan || 1}\" \r\n                        style=\"background-color: ${colors[index]}; _**Agent (composer-1)**_


Heatmap colors come from two places:

## Backend (Python) — primary source for tables

Heatmap colors are generated in the backend and sent to the frontend via `cell_metadata`:

1. Location: `app/utils/color_constants.py`
   - `get_heat_map_color()` function (lines 75-121)
   - Default colors (when `use_low_high=True`):
     - Low value: `heat_map_low` = `#d9596a` (red/pink)
     - High value: `heat_map_high` = `#1b8da7` (blue/teal)
   - Alternative colors (when `use_low_high=False`):
     - Start: `heat_map_start` = `#dddddd` (light gray)
     - End: `heat_map_end` = `#1b8da7` (blue/teal)

2. Application: `app/utils/league_utils.py`
   - `apply_heat_map_to_columns()` calls `get_heat_map_color()` for each cell
   - Colors are stored in `cell_metadata` as `{"backgroundColor": "rgb(...)"}`
   - This metadata is included in the `TableData` sent to the frontend

3. Frontend rendering: `app/templates/components/tables.html`
   - Reads colors from `cellMetadataMap[rowIdx][field]`
   - Applies them as inline styles with `!important` to override striping

## Frontend JavaScript — used for Bootstrap tables

There's also a JavaScript function that's not used for Tabulator tables:

- Location: `app/static/js/theme/color-utils.js`
- Function: `getHeatMapColor()` (line 258)
- Default colors (hardcoded, not using theme colors):
  - Start: `#2a6fdb` (blue)
  - End: `#d1495b` (red)
- Used only in: Bootstrap table rendering (line 1926 in `tables.html`)

## Summary

For Tabulator tables (most tables), heatmap colors come from the backend:
- Low values: `#d9596a` (red/pink)
- High values: `#1b8da7` (blue/teal)

The frontend `getHeatMapColor()` function has different defaults and is only used for Bootstrap tables, not Tabulator tables.

Should I align the frontend function with the backend colors, or update the backend to use different colors?

---

_**User (2025-12-12 08:49:15Z)**_

id like to not deal with colors in the backend at all. remove the mark up from the backend and make tabular capable of using the heatmap colors defined n the frontend

---

        "contents": "function renderTeamVsTeamComparison(data, options) {\n\r\n\r\n    ...\n\r\n\r\n    // Create table using Tabulator\r\n    if (typeof createTableTabulator === 'function') {\r\n        const tableOptions = {\r\n            disablePositionCircle,\r\n            enableSpecialRowStyling: true,\r\n            tooltips: true,\r\n            enableHeatMap\r\n        };\r\n        \r\n        if (teamField) {\r\n            tableOptions.teamField = teamField;\r\n        }\r\n        \r\n        createTableTabulator(tableId, data, tableOptions);\r\n    } else if (typeof createTableBootstrap3 === 'function') {\r\n        // Fallback to Bootstrap\r\n        createTableBootstrap3(tableId, data, {\r\n            disablePositionCircle,\r\n            enableSpecialRowStyling: true,\r\n            enableHeatMap\r\n        });\r\n    } else {\r\n        console.error('Table creation function not available');\r\n    }\r\n}",

        "originalContents": "\r\n\r\n    // Create table using Tabulator\r\n    if (typeof createTableTabulator === 'function') {\r\n        const tableOptions = {\r\n            disablePositionCircle,\r\n            enableSpecialRowStyling: true,\r\n            tooltips: true,\r\n            enableHeatMap\r\n        };\r\n        \r\n        if (teamField) {\r\n            tableOptions.teamField = teamField;\r\n        }\r\n        \r\n        createTableTabulator(tableId, data, tableOptions);\r\n    } else if (typeof createTableBootstrap3 === 'function') {\r\n        // Fallback to Bootstrap\r\n        createTableBootstrap3(tableId, data, {\r\n            disablePositionCircle,\r\n            enableSpecialRowStyling: true,\r\n            enableHeatMap\r\n        });\r\n    } else {\r\n        console.error('Table creation function not available');\r\n    }\r\n}",
        "range": {

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "function createTableTabulator(tableId, data, options = {}) {\r\n    \r\n    const settings = {\r\n        disablePositionCircle: false,\r\n        enableSpecialRowStyling: false,\r\n        tooltips: true,\r\n        teamField: null,\r\n        ...options\r\n    };\r\n    const tableConfig = data.config || {};\r\n    const isCompactLayout = tableConfig.compact === true;\r\n    const useStripedGroups = tableConfig.stripedColGroups === true;\r\n    // Highlighting style constants\r\n    const HIGHLIGHT_STYLES = {\r\n        headerBackgroundColor: 'rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05)',\r\n        cellBackgroundColor: 'rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.03)',\r\n        headerFontWeight: '900',\r\n        cellFontWeight: '700'\r\n    };\r\n\r\n    if (!data || !data.columns || !data.data) {\r\n        console.error('Invalid data format for Tabulator table');\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    const container = document.getElementById(tableId);\r\n    if (!container) {\r\n        console.error(&#96;Container \"${tableId}\" not found for Tabulator table&#96;);\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n    \r\n    // Ensure container takes full width\r\n    if (container.style.width === '') {\r\n        container.style.width = '100%';\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    const columnOrder = flattenColumnMetadata(data.columns);\r\n    if (!columnOrder.length) {\r\n        console.warn('No column definitions available for Tabulator table');\r\n        const container = document.getElementById(tableId);\r\n        if (container) {\r\n            const errorMsg = data.title \u0026\u0026 data.title.includes('Fehler') ? data.title : 'No column definitions available';\r\n            container.innerHTML = &#96;\u003cdiv class=\"alert alert-warning\"\u003e${errorMsg}\u003c/div\u003e&#96;;\r\n        }\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n    const columnLookup = columnOrder.reduce((acc, info) =\u003e {\r\n        acc[&#96;${info.groupIndex}-${info.columnIndex}&#96;] = info;\r\n        return acc;\r\n    }, {});\r\n\r\n    const teamFieldGuess = data.columns?.[0]?.columns?.[1]?.field || null;\r\n    const teamField = settings.teamField || teamFieldGuess;\r\n",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 159,
            "text": "    return Number.isNaN(parsed) ? undefined : parsed;\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 160,
            "text": "}\r"

            "lineNumber": 161,
            "text": "\r"

            "lineNumber": 164,
            "text": "    const settings = {\r"

            "lineNumber": 171,

          },

          {
            "lineNumber": 174,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 175,
            "text": "    const HIGHLIGHT_STYLES = {\r"

          }
        ],
        "originalContents": "function createTableTabulator(tableId, data, options = {}) {\r\n    \r\n    const settings = {\r\n        disablePositionCircle: false,\r\n        enableSpecialRowStyling: false,\r\n        tooltips: true,\r\n        teamField: null,\r\n        ...options\r\n    };\r\n    const tableConfig = data.config || {};\r\n    const isCompactLayout = tableConfig.compact === true;\r\n    const useStripedGroups = tableConfig.stripedColGroups === true;\r\n    // Highlighting style constants\r\n    const HIGHLIGHT_STYLES = {\r\n        headerBackgroundColor: 'rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05)',\r\n        cellBackgroundColor: 'rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.03)',\r\n        headerFontWeight: '900',\r\n        cellFontWeight: '700'\r\n    };\r\n\r\n    if (!data || !data.columns || !data.data) {\r\n        console.error('Invalid data format for Tabulator table');\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    const container = document.getElementById(tableId);\r\n    if (!container) {\r\n        console.error(&#96;Container \"${tableId}\" not found for Tabulator table&#96;);\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n    \r\n    // Ensure container takes full width\r\n    if (container.style.width === '') {\r\n        container.style.width = '100%';\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    const columnOrder = flattenColumnMetadata(data.columns);\r\n    if (!columnOrder.length) {\r\n        console.warn('No column definitions available for Tabulator table');\r\n        const container = document.getElementById(tableId);\r\n        if (container) {\r\n            const errorMsg = data.title \u0026\u0026 data.title.includes('Fehler') ? data.title : 'No column definitions available';\r\n            container.innerHTML = &#96;\u003cdiv class=\"alert alert-warning\"\u003e${errorMsg}\u003c/div\u003e&#96;;\r\n        }\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n    const columnLookup = columnOrder.reduce((acc, info) =\u003e {\r\n        acc[&#96;${info.groupIndex}-${info.columnIndex}&#96;] = info;\r\n        return acc;\r\n    }, {});\r\n\r\n    const teamFieldGuess = data.columns?.[0]?.columns?.[1]?.field || null;\r\n    const teamField = settings.teamField || teamFieldGuess;\r\n",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 216
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 158
          }

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "                if (field.startsWith('week_')) {\r\n                    console.log(&#96;[Decimal Debug] Field: \"${field}\", decimalPlaces: ${decimalPlaces}, column.decimal_places: ${column.decimal_places}, column keys:&#96;, Object.keys(column));\r\n                }\r\n                \r\n                const formatter = function(cell) {\r\n                    const value = cell.getValue();\r\n                    const element = cell.getElement();\r\n                    const rowData = cell.getRow().getData();\r\n                    const rowIdx = rowData.__rowIndex;\r\n                    const row = cell.getRow();\r\n\r\n                    // Add highlighting class and style to cell element if group is highlighted\r\n                    if (isHighlighted) {\r\n                        element.classList.add('tab-col-highlighted');\r\n                        element.style.setProperty('font-weight', HIGHLIGHT_STYLES.cellFontWeight, 'important');\r\n                    }\r\n\r\n                    // Column-level inline styles\r\n                    applyElementStyles(element, columnStyles);\r\n\r\n                    // Cell metadata styles (heatmap colors) - apply with !important to override striping\r\n                    const cellMeta = cellMetadataMap[rowIdx]?.[field];\r\n                    if (cellMeta) {\r\n                        // Apply heatmap background color with !important to override striping CSS\r\n                        if (cellMeta.backgroundColor) {\r\n                            element.style.setProperty('background-color', cellMeta.backgroundColor, 'important');\r\n                        }\r\n                        // Apply other styles normally\r\n                        Object.entries(cellMeta).forEach(([property, value]) =\u003e {\r\n                            if (property !== 'backgroundColor' \u0026\u0026 value !== undefined \u0026\u0026 value !== null) {\r\n                                element.style[property] = value;\r\n                            }\r\n                        });\r\n                    }\r\n",

        "contents": "\r\n    const tabulatorOptions = {\r\n        data: transformedData,\r\n        columns: tabulatorColumns,\r\n        layout: isCompactLayout ? \"fitData\" : \"fitColumns\",\r\n        responsiveLayout: false,\r\n        pagination: false,\r\n        movableColumns: false,\r\n        tooltips: tooltipOption,\r\n        height: \"auto\",\r\n        rowHeight: 40,\r\n        theme: \"semanticui\",\r\n        cssClass: \"custom-tabulator\",\r\n        columnHeaderVertAlign: \"middle\",\r\n        width: \"100%\", // Ensure table takes full width of container\r\n        ...(initialSort ? { initialSort: initialSort } : {}),\r\n        tableBuilt: function() {\r\n            try {\r\n                // Apply highlighting and fix header separator\r\n                // Use requestAnimationFrame for better timing\r\n                requestAnimationFrame(() =\u003e {\r\n                    requestAnimationFrame(() =\u003e {\r\n                    const tableElement = document.querySelector(&#96;#${tableId}&#96;);\r\n                    if (!tableElement) {\r\n                        return;\r\n                    }\r\n                    \r\n                    // Set CSS custom properties for highlighting styles\r\n                    tableElement.style.setProperty('--highlight-header-bg', HIGHLIGHT_STYLES.headerBackgroundColor);\r\n                    tableElement.style.setProperty('--highlight-cell-bg', HIGHLIGHT_STYLES.cellBackgroundColor);\r\n                    tableElement.style.setProperty('--highlight-header-weight', HIGHLIGHT_STYLES.headerFontWeight);\r\n                    tableElement.style.setProperty('--highlight-cell-weight', HIGHLIGHT_STYLES.cellFontWeight);\r\n                    \r\n                    // Stripe coloring: assign group classes to column group headers\r\n                    // (cells and column headers get classes via cssClass/headerClass in column definitions)\r\n",

        "originalContents": "\r\n    const tabulatorOptions = {\r\n        data: transformedData,\r\n        columns: tabulatorColumns,\r\n        layout: isCompactLayout ? \"fitData\" : \"fitColumns\",\r\n        responsiveLayout: false,\r\n        pagination: false,\r\n        movableColumns: false,\r\n        tooltips: tooltipOption,\r\n        height: \"auto\",\r\n        rowHeight: 40,\r\n        theme: \"semanticui\",\r\n        cssClass: \"custom-tabulator\",\r\n        columnHeaderVertAlign: \"middle\",\r\n        width: \"100%\", // Ensure table takes full width of container\r\n        ...(initialSort ? { initialSort: initialSort } : {}),\r\n        tableBuilt: function() {\r\n            try {\r\n                // Apply highlighting and fix header separator\r\n                // Use requestAnimationFrame for better timing\r\n                requestAnimationFrame(() =\u003e {\r\n                    requestAnimationFrame(() =\u003e {\r\n                    const tableElement = document.querySelector(&#96;#${tableId}&#96;);\r\n                    if (!tableElement) {\r\n                        return;\r\n                    }\r\n                    \r\n                    // Set CSS custom properties for highlighting styles\r\n                    tableElement.style.setProperty('--highlight-header-bg', HIGHLIGHT_STYLES.headerBackgroundColor);\r\n                    tableElement.style.setProperty('--highlight-cell-bg', HIGHLIGHT_STYLES.cellBackgroundColor);\r\n                    tableElement.style.setProperty('--highlight-header-weight', HIGHLIGHT_STYLES.headerFontWeight);\r\n                    tableElement.style.setProperty('--highlight-cell-weight', HIGHLIGHT_STYLES.cellFontWeight);\r\n                    \r\n                    // Stripe coloring: assign group classes to column group headers\r\n                    // (cells and column headers get classes via cssClass/headerClass in column definitions)\r\n",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\r\n\r\n\r\ndef apply_heat_map_to_columns(table_data: List[List], cell_metadata: Dict[str, Dict],\r\n                              column_indices: List[int], min_val: float = None, max_val: float = None) -\u003e Dict[str, Dict]:\r\n    \"\"\"\r\n    Apply heat map coloring to specified column indices.\r\n    \r\n    Args:\r\n        table_data: List of rows, where each row is a list of values\r\n        cell_metadata: Dictionary mapping \"row:col\" to cell metadata\r\n        column_indices: List of column indices (0-based) to apply coloring to\r\n        min_val: Optional minimum value for color scale. If None, calculated from data\r\n        max_val: Optional maximum value for color scale. If None, calculated from data\r\n        \r\n    Returns:\r\n        Updated cell_metadata dictionary\r\n    \"\"\"\r\n    if not table_data or not column_indices:\r\n        return cell_metadata\r\n    \r\n    # Extract all values from specified columns\r\n    all_values = []\r\n    for row in table_data:\r\n        for col_idx in column_indices:\r\n            if col_idx \u003c len(row):\r\n                value = row[col_idx]\r\n                # Only include numeric values (skip empty strings, None, etc.)\r\n                if isinstance(value, (int, float)) and value != \"\":\r\n                    all_values.append(value)\r\n    \r\n    if not all_values:\r\n        return cell_metadata\r\n    \r\n    # Calculate min/max if not provided\r\n    calculated_min = min(all_values) if min_val is None else min_val\r\n    calculated_max = max(all_values) if max_val is None else max_val\r\n    \r\n    # Apply coloring to each cell in specified columns\r\n    for row_idx, row in enumerate(table_data):\r\n        for col_idx in column_indices:\r\n            if col_idx \u003c len(row):\r\n                value = row[col_idx]\r\n                if isinstance(value, (int, float)) and value != \"\":\r\n                    color = get_heat_map_color(value, calculated_min, calculated_max)\r\n                    cell_metadata[f\"{row_idx}:{col_idx}\"] = {\"backgroundColor\": color}\r\n    \r\n    return cell_metadata",

          {
            "lineNumber": 93,
            "text": "    calculated_max = max(all_values) if max_val is None else max_val\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 94,
            "text": "    \r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 95,
            "text": "    # Apply coloring to each cell in specified columns\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 96,
            "text": "    for row_idx, row in enumerate(table_data):\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 97,
            "text": "        for col_idx in column_indices:\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 98,
            "text": "            if col_idx \u003c len(row):\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 99,
            "text": "                value = row[col_idx]\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 100,
            "text": "                if isinstance(value, (int, float)) and value != \"\":\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 101,
            "text": "                    color = get_heat_map_color(value, calculated_min, calculated_max)\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 102,
            "text": "                    cell_metadata[f\"{row_idx}:{col_idx}\"] = {\"backgroundColor\": color}\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 103,
            "text": "    \r"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 104,
            "text": "    return cell_metadata"
          }
        ],
        "originalContents": "\r\n\r\n\r\ndef apply_heat_map_to_columns(table_data: List[List], cell_metadata: Dict[str, Dict],\r\n                              column_indices: List[int], min_val: float = None, max_val: float = None) -\u003e Dict[str, Dict]:\r\n    \"\"\"\r\n    Apply heat map coloring to specified column indices.\r\n    \r\n    Args:\r\n        table_data: List of rows, where each row is a list of values\r\n        cell_metadata: Dictionary mapping \"row:col\" to cell metadata\r\n        column_indices: List of column indices (0-based) to apply coloring to\r\n        min_val: Optional minimum value for color scale. If None, calculated from data\r\n        max_val: Optional maximum value for color scale. If None, calculated from data\r\n        \r\n    Returns:\r\n        Updated cell_metadata dictionary\r\n    \"\"\"\r\n    if not table_data or not column_indices:\r\n        return cell_metadata\r\n    \r\n    # Extract all values from specified columns\r\n    all_values = []\r\n    for row in table_data:\r\n        for col_idx in column_indices:\r\n            if col_idx \u003c len(row):\r\n                value = row[col_idx]\r\n                # Only include numeric values (skip empty strings, None, etc.)\r\n                if isinstance(value, (int, float)) and value != \"\":\r\n                    all_values.append(value)\r\n    \r\n    if not all_values:\r\n        return cell_metadata\r\n    \r\n    # Calculate min/max if not provided\r\n    calculated_min = min(all_values) if min_val is None else min_val\r\n    calculated_max = max(all_values) if max_val is None else max_val\r\n    \r\n    # Apply coloring to each cell in specified columns\r\n    for row_idx, row in enumerate(table_data):\r\n        for col_idx in column_indices:\r\n            if col_idx \u003c len(row):\r\n                value = row[col_idx]\r\n                if isinstance(value, (int, float)) and value != \"\":\r\n                    color = get_heat_map_color(value, calculated_min, calculated_max)\r\n                    cell_metadata[f\"{row_idx}:{col_idx}\"] = {\"backgroundColor\": color}\r\n    \r\n    return cell_metadata",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "column": 24,
            "line": 103
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 37
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "app/utils/league_utils.py"

        "contents": "class LeagueService:\n    ...\n    def get_game_overview_data(self, season: str, league: str, week: int, round_number: int) -\u003e TableData:\n\n                \n                ...\n\n                \n                data.append([\n                    team_position if team_position is not None else '',\n                    team_name,\n                    team_pins,\n                    total_team_points,\n                    total_opponent_points,\n                    opponent_pins,\n                    opponent_position if opponent_position is not None else '',\n                    opponent_name\n                ])\n                \n                processed_teams.add(team_name)\n                processed_teams.add(opponent_name)\n            \n            # Apply heatmap coloring to points and pins columns\n            cell_metadata = {}\n            # Pins columns: team_pins (index 2) and opponent_pins (index 5)\n            apply_heat_map_to_columns(data, cell_metadata, [2, 5])\n            # Points columns: team_points (index 3) and opponent_points (index 4)\n            apply_heat_map_to_columns(data, cell_metadata, [3, 4])\n            \n            return TableData(\n                columns=columns,\n                data=data,\n                title=f\"{league} - {i18n_service.get_text('week')} {week}, {i18n_service.get_text('game')} {round_number}\",\n                description=f\"{i18n_service.get_text('match_results')} {season}\",\n                config={\n                    \"stickyHeader\": True,\n                    \"striped\": True,\n                    \"hover\": True,\n                    \"responsive\": True,\n                    \"compact\": False,\n                    \"stripedColGroups\": True\n                },\n                cell_metadata=cell_metadata\n            )\n            \n        except Exception as e:\n            print(f\"Error in get_game_overview_data: {e}\")\n            import traceback\n            traceback.print_exc()\n            return TableData(\n                columns=[],\n                data=[],\n                title=f\"Error loading game overview data\"\n            )",

        "contents": "\r\n                    // Cell metadata styles (heatmap colors) - apply with !important to override striping\r\n                    const cellMeta = cellMetadataMap[rowIdx]?.[field];\r\n                    if (cellMeta) {\r\n                        // Apply heatmap background color with !important to override striping CSS\r\n                        if (cellMeta.backgroundColor) {\r\n                            element.style.setProperty('background-color', cellMeta.backgroundColor, 'important');\r\n                        }\r\n                        // Apply other styles normally\r\n                        Object.entries(cellMeta).forEach(([property, value]) =\u003e {\r\n                            if (property !== 'backgroundColor' \u0026\u0026 value !== undefined \u0026\u0026 value !== null) {\r\n                                element.style[property] = value;\r\n                            }\r\n                        });\r\n                    }\r\n",

        "originalContents": "\r\n                    // Cell metadata styles (heatmap colors) - apply with !important to override striping\r\n                    const cellMeta = cellMetadataMap[rowIdx]?.[field];\r\n                    if (cellMeta) {\r\n                        // Apply heatmap background color with !important to override striping CSS\r\n                        if (cellMeta.backgroundColor) {\r\n                            element.style.setProperty('background-color', cellMeta.backgroundColor, 'important');\r\n                        }\r\n                        // Apply other styles normally\r\n                        Object.entries(cellMeta).forEach(([property, value]) =\u003e {\r\n                            if (property !== 'backgroundColor' \u0026\u0026 value !== undefined \u0026\u0026 value !== null) {\r\n                                element.style[property] = value;\r\n                            }\r\n                        });\r\n                    }\r\n",
