t('league', 'League') : 'League') },\r\n                { title: (typeof t === 'function' ? t('ui.comparison.difference', 'Difference') : 'Difference') }\r\n            ],\r\n            headerGroups: []\r\n        };",

        "originalContents": "\r\n    \r\n    /**\r\n     * Render the comparison table\r\n     */\r\n    renderComparisonTable(data) {\r\n        const tableContainer = document.getElementById('tableLeagueComparison');\r\n        if (!tableContainer) {\r\n            console.warn('Table container not found, skipping table render');\r\n            return;\r\n        }\r\n        \r\n        const seasons = Object.keys(data).sort();\r\n        \r\n        // Create comparison table data\r\n        const tableData = seasons.map(season =\u003e {\r\n            const seasonData = data[season];\r\n            return [\r\n                season,\r\n                seasonData.league_name,\r\n                seasonData.performance_rank,\r\n                seasonData.team_performance.team_average_score,\r\n                seasonData.league_averages.average_score,\r\n                seasonData.team_performance.vs_league_score \u003e 0 ? \r\n                    &#96;+${seasonData.team_performance.vs_league_score}&#96; : \r\n                    seasonData.team_performance.vs_league_score\r\n            ];\r\n        });\r\n\r\n        const tableConfig = {\r\n            data: tableData,\r\n            columns: [\r\n                { title: (typeof t === 'function' ? t('season', 'Season') : 'Season') },\r\n                { title: (typeof t === 'function' ? t('league', 'League') : 'League') },\r\n                { title: (typeof t === 'function' ? t('ranking', 'Ranking') : 'Ranking') },\r\n                { title: (typeof t === 'function' ? t('average', 'Avg') : 'Avg') + ' - ' + (typeof t === 'function' ? t('team', 'Team') : 'Team') },\r\n                { title: (typeof t === 'function' ? t('average', 'Avg') : 'Avg') + ' - ' + (typeof t === 'function' ? t('league', 'League') : 'League') },\r\n                { title: (typeof t === 'function' ? t('ui.comparison.difference', 'Difference') : 'Difference') }\r\n            ],\r\n            headerGroups: []\r\n        };",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\"\"\"\r\nCompare input CSV with reconstructed CSV, ignoring row ordering.\r\n\r\nThis script loads both files, normalizes them, and reports differences.\r\n\"\"\"\r\n\r\nimport sys\r\nfrom pathlib import Path\r\nimport pandas as pd\r\n\r\nROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]\r\nINPUT_CSV = ROOT / \"database\" / \"data\" / \"bowling_ergebnisse_real.csv\"\r\nOUTPUT_CSV = ROOT / \"database\" / \"data\" / \"bowling_ergebnisse_reconstructed.csv\"\r\n\r\n\r\ndef normalize_dataframe(df: pd.DataFrame, name: str) -\u003e pd.DataFrame:\r\n\t\"\"\"Normalize a DataFrame for comparison.\"\"\"\r\n\tprint(f\"Normalizing {name}: {len(df)} rows, {len(df.columns)} columns\")\r\n\t\r\n\t# Make a copy\r\n\tdf = df.copy()\r\n\t\r\n\t# Define meaningful sort order (matching the original CSV structure)\r\n\tsort_columns = [\r\n\t\t\"Season\", \"League\", \"Week\", \"Date\", \"Round Number\", \r\n\t\t\"Team\", \"Position\", \"Player\", \"Player ID\", \"Opponent\" #, \"Match Number\"\r\n\t]\r\n\t\r\n\t# Use only columns that exist in the DataFrame\r\n\tavailable_sort_cols = [col for col in sort_columns if col in df.columns]\r\n\tif not available_sort_cols:\r\n\t\t# Fallback: use all columns if none of the expected columns exist\r\n\t\tavailable_sort_cols = df.columns.tolist()\r\n\t\r\n\t# Sort by meaningful columns, handling numeric columns properly\r\n\tdef numeric_sort_key(col_series):\r\n\t\tcol_name = col_series.name\r\n\t\tif col_name in [\"Week\", \"Round Number\", \"Match Number\", \"Position\"]:\r\n\t\t\treturn pd.to_numeric(col_series, errors=\"coerce\").fillna(0)\r\n\t\treturn col_series\r\n\t\r\n\tdf = df.sort_values(\r\n\t\tby=available_sort_cols,\r\n\t\tkey=numeric_sort_key\r\n\t).reset_index(drop=True)",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "\"\"\"\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2,
            "text": "Compare input CSV with reconstructed CSV, ignoring row ordering.\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "This script loads both files, normalizes them, and reports differences.\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "\"\"\"\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "import sys\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "from pathlib import Path\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "import pandas as pd\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12,
            "text": "INPUT_CSV = ROOT / \"database\" / \"data\" / \"bowling_ergebnisse_real.csv\"\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13,
            "text": "OUTPUT_CSV = ROOT / \"database\" / \"data\" / \"bowling_ergebnisse_reconstructed.csv\"\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "def normalize_dataframe(df: pd.DataFrame, name: str) -\u003e pd.DataFrame:\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17,
            "text": "\t\"\"\"Normalize a DataFrame for comparison.\"\"\"\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "\tprint(f\"Normalizing {name}: {len(df)} rows, {len(df.columns)} columns\")\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19,
            "text": "\t\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "\t# Make a copy\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21,
            "text": "\tdf = df.copy()\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "\t\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23,
            "text": "\t# Define meaningful sort order (matching the original CSV structure)\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "\tsort_columns = [\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25,
            "text": "\t\t\"Season\", \"League\", \"Week\", \"Date\", \"Round Number\", \r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "\t\t\"Team\", \"Position\", \"Player\", \"Player ID\", \"Opponent\" #, \"Match Number\"\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27,
            "text": "\t]\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "\t\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29,
            "text": "\t# Use only columns that exist in the DataFrame\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "text": "\tavailable_sort_cols = [col for col in sort_columns if col in df.columns]\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31,
            "text": "\tif not available_sort_cols:\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32,
            "text": "\t\t# Fallback: use all columns if none of the expected columns exist\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33,
            "text": "\t\tavailable_sort_cols = df.columns.tolist()\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34,
            "text": "\t\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35,
            "text": "\t# Sort by meaningful columns, handling numeric columns properly\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36,
            "text": "\tdef numeric_sort_key(col_series):\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37,
            "text": "\t\tcol_name = col_series.name\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38,
            "text": "\t\tif col_name in [\"Week\", \"Round Number\", \"Match Number\", \"Position\"]:\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39,
            "text": "\t\t\treturn pd.to_numeric(col_series, errors=\"coerce\").fillna(0)\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40,
            "text": "\t\treturn col_series\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41,
            "text": "\t\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 42,
            "text": "\tdf = df.sort_values(\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 43,
            "text": "\t\tby=available_sort_cols,\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 44,
            "text": "\t\tkey=numeric_sort_key\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 45,
            "text": "\t).reset_index(drop=True)\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 46,
            "text": "\t\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 47,
            "text": "\t# Convert all columns to string for comparison (handles NaN and type differences)\r"
          }
        ],
        "originalContents": "\"\"\"\r\nCompare input CSV with reconstructed CSV, ignoring row ordering.\r\n\r\nThis script loads both files, normalizes them, and reports differences.\r\n\"\"\"\r\n\r\nimport sys\r\nfrom pathlib import Path\r\nimport pandas as pd\r\n\r\nROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]\r\nINPUT_CSV = ROOT / \"database\" / \"data\" / \"bowling_ergebnisse_real.csv\"\r\nOUTPUT_CSV = ROOT / \"database\" / \"data\" / \"bowling_ergebnisse_reconstructed.csv\"\r\n\r\n\r\ndef normalize_dataframe(df: pd.DataFrame, name: str) -\u003e pd.DataFrame:\r\n\t\"\"\"Normalize a DataFrame for comparison.\"\"\"\r\n\tprint(f\"Normalizing {name}: {len(df)} rows, {len(df.columns)} columns\")\r\n\t\r\n\t# Make a copy\r\n\tdf = df.copy()\r\n\t\r\n\t# Define meaningful sort order (matching the original CSV structure)\r\n\tsort_columns = [\r\n\t\t\"Season\", \"League\", \"Week\", \"Date\", \"Round Number\", \r\n\t\t\"Team\", \"Position\", \"Player\", \"Player ID\", \"Opponent\" #, \"Match Number\"\r\n\t]\r\n\t\r\n\t# Use only columns that exist in the DataFrame\r\n\tavailable_sort_cols = [col for col in sort_columns if col in df.columns]\r\n\tif not available_sort_cols:\r\n\t\t# Fallback: use all columns if none of the expected columns exist\r\n\t\tavailable_sort_cols = df.columns.tolist()\r\n\t\r\n\t# Sort by meaningful columns, handling numeric columns properly\r\n\tdef numeric_sort_key(col_series):\r\n\t\tcol_name = col_series.name\r\n\t\tif col_name in [\"Week\", \"Round Number\", \"Match Number\", \"Position\"]:\r\n\t\t\treturn pd.to_numeric(col_series, errors=\"coerce\").fillna(0)\r\n\t\treturn col_series\r\n\t\r\n\tdf = df.sort_values(\r\n\t\tby=available_sort_cols,\r\n\t\tkey=numeric_sort_key\r\n\t).reset_index(drop=True)",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 68
          },

        },

        "contents": "def compare_dataframes(df1: pd.DataFrame, df2: pd.DataFrame, name1: str, name2: str) -\u003e bool:\n\r\n\t\r\n\t...\n\r\n\t\r\n\t# Compare row by row\r\n\tif len1 == len2:\r\n\t\t# Sort both by meaningful columns (same as normalization)\r\n\t\tsort_columns = [\r\n\t\t\t\"Season\", \"League\", \"Week\", \"Date\", \"Round Number\",\r\n\t\t\t\"Team\", \"Position\", \"Player\", \"Player ID\", \"Opponent\"\r\n\t\t]\r\n\t\tavailable_sort_cols = [col for col in sort_columns if col in common_cols]\r\n\t\tif not available_sort_cols:\r\n\t\t\tavailable_sort_cols = common_cols[:min(10, len(common_cols))]  # Use first 10 columns as fallback\r\n\t\t\r\n\t\tdef numeric_sort_key(col_series):\r\n\t\t\tcol_name = col_series.name\r\n\t\t\tif col_name in [\"Week\", \"Round Number\", \"Position\"]:\r\n\t\t\t\treturn pd.to_numeric(col_series, errors=\"coerce\").fillna(0)\r\n\t\t\treturn col_series\r\n\t\t\r\n\t\tdf1_sorted = df1.sort_values(\r\n\t\t\tby=available_sort_cols,\r\n\t\t\tkey=numeric_sort_key\r\n\t\t).reset_index(drop=True)\r\n\t\tdf2_sorted = df2.sort_values(\r\n\t\t\tby=available_sort_cols,\r\n\t\t\tkey=numeric_sort_key\r\n\t\t).reset_index(drop=True)\r\n\t\t\r\n\t\t# Compare\r\n\t\tdiffs = df1_sorted != df2_sorted",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "# Team Stats Enhancement Plan\r\n\r\n## Overview\r\nThis document outlines the complete implementation plan for enhancing the team statistics page with advanced analytics and insights.\r\n\r\n## Phase 0: Foundation \u0026 Documentation\r\n- [ ] Create comprehensive data glossary and definitions\r\n- [ ] Document database schema and column meanings  \r\n- [ ] Define key metrics and calculation methods\r\n- [ ] Establish common terminology across team/player/league contexts\r\n- [ ] Document data types (input_data vs computed_data, individual vs team totals)\r\n## Phase 1: League Comparison + History ✅ COMPLETED\r\n\r\n### ✅ Backend Implementation\r\n- [x] Add &#96;get_league_comparison_data()&#96; method to TeamService\r\n- [x] Add &#96;get_league_averages()&#96; method to TeamService  \r\n- [x] Add &#96;get_team_vs_league_performance()&#96; method to TeamService\r\n- [x] Add &#96;/team/get_league_comparison&#96; API endpoint\r\n\r\n### ✅ Frontend Implementation\r\n- [x] Add \"Leistung vs. Liga-Durchschnitt\" section to team stats page\r\n- [x] Create area chart showing team vs league average with conditional coloring\r\n- [x] Create league comparison table with detailed metrics (reordered columns)\r\n- [x] Add &#96;updateLeagueComparison()&#96; JavaScript function\r\n- [x] Integrate with team selection handler\r\n- [x] Remove deprecated \"Durchschnittliche Leistung pro Saison\" chart\r\n- [x] Clean up deprecated backend functions and routes\r\n\r\n### ✅ Features Delivered\r\n- League average scores and points per season (using individual data)\r\n- Team performance vs league average (difference, Z-scores)\r\n- Historical league strength context\r\n- Visual area chart with green/red coloring for performance gaps\r\n- Clean, focused layout with full-width team history chart\r\n- Proper column ordering in comparison table\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n## Phase 2: Clutch \u0026 Consistency ✅ COMPLETED\r\n\r\n### ✅ Backend Implementation\r\n- [x] Add &#96;get_clutch_performance()&#96; method to TeamService\r\n- [x] Add &#96;get_consistency_metrics()&#96; method to TeamService\r\n",
        "detailedLines": [

            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "# Team Stats Enhancement Plan\r"

            "lineNumber": 2,

            "text": "## Overview\r"

_**Agent (composer-1)**_


Updated the sorting logic. The tuple approach is correct, but ensuring both values are numeric and handling edge cases improves reliability.

**Changes made:**

1. **Explicit type conversion**: Converted both `season_points` and `season_score` to `float()` to ensure consistent numeric comparison
2. **None handling**: Added `or 0` fallback to handle any None values
3. **Cleanup**: Removed commented-out code

The sorting now works as follows:
- **Primary sort**: `season_points` (descending)
- **Tiebreaker**: `season_score` (descending) when `season_points` are equal

The tuple `(float(season_points), float(season_score))` ensures Python compares first by points, then by score when points are equal. The `reverse=True` parameter sorts both criteria in descending order.

This should correctly sort teams by points first, then by score as the tiebreaker.

---

```json
{
  "codeResults": [
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "class LeagueService:\n    ...\n    def get_game_team_details_data(self, season: str, league: str, week: int, team: str, round_number: int) -\u003e TableData:\n\n        ...\n\n        for team_name, team_stats in stats.team_stats.items():\n            # Get the latest weekly performance\n            latest_week = max(team_stats.weekly_performances.keys())\n            week_perf = team_stats.weekly_performances[latest_week]\n            \n            # Create weekly performances list\n            weekly_performances = []\n            for week_num, perf in team_stats.weekly_performances.items():\n                weekly_performances.append(\n                    TeamWeeklyPerformance(\n                        team_id=perf.team_id,\n                        team_name=perf.team_name,\n                        week=week_num,\n                        score=perf.total_score,\n                        number_of_games=perf.number_of_games,\n                        points=perf.points\n                    )\n                )\n            \n            # Sort weekly performances by week\n            weekly_performances.sort(key=lambda x: x.week)\n            \n            # Create team performance\n            team_performances.append(\n                TeamSeasonPerformance(\n                    team_id=week_perf.team_id,\n                    team_name=team_name,\n                    total_score=team_stats.season_summary.total_score,\n                    total_points=team_stats.season_summary.total_points,\n                    average=team_stats.season_summary.average_score,\n                    weekly_performances=weekly_performances\n                )\n            )\n        \n        # Sort by total points (descending) and assign positions\n        team_performances.sort(key=lambda x: (x.total_points, x.total_score), reverse=True)\n        for i, perf in enumerate(team_performances, 1):\n            perf.position = i\n        \n        # Create and return the LeagueStandings\n        return LeagueStandings(\n            season=season,\n            league_name=league,\n            week=week,\n            teams=team_performances,\n            last_updated=datetime.datetime.now().isoformat()\n        )",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 42,
            "text": "class LeagueService:"
          },

        ],
        "originalContents": "\n        for team_name, team_stats in stats.team_stats.items():\n            # Get the latest weekly performance\n            latest_week = max(team_stats.weekly_performances.keys())\n            week_perf = team_stats.weekly_performances[latest_week]\n            \n            # Create weekly performances list\n            weekly_performances = []\n            for week_num, perf in team_stats.weekly_performances.items():\n                weekly_performances.append(\n                    TeamWeeklyPerformance(\n                        team_id=perf.team_id,\n                        team_name=perf.team_name,\n                        week=week_num,\n                        score=perf.total_score,\n                        number_of_games=perf.number_of_games,\n                        points=perf.points\n                    )\n                )\n            \n            # Sort weekly performances by week\n            weekly_performances.sort(key=lambda x: x.week)\n            \n            # Create team performance\n            team_performances.append(\n                TeamSeasonPerformance(\n                    team_id=week_perf.team_id,\n                    team_name=team_name,\n                    total_score=team_stats.season_summary.total_score,\n                    total_points=team_stats.season_summary.total_points,\n                    average=team_stats.season_summary.average_score,\n                    weekly_performances=weekly_performances\n                )\n            )\n        \n        # Sort by total points (descending) and assign positions\n        team_performances.sort(key=lambda x: (x.total_points, x.total_score), reverse=True)\n        for i, perf in enumerate(team_performances, 1):\n            perf.position = i\n        \n        # Create and return the LeagueStandings\n        return LeagueStandings(\n            season=season,\n            league_name=league,\n            week=week,\n            teams=team_performances,\n            last_updated=datetime.datetime.now().isoformat()\n        )",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\r\n\r\n/**\r\n * Render league standings table\r\n * @param {string} tableId - ID of the container element\r\n * @param {Object} standingsData - TableData object from backend\r\n * @param {Object} options - Additional options\r\n */\r\nfunction renderLeagueStandingsTable(tableId, standingsData, options = {}) {\r\n    const container = document.getElementById(tableId);\r\n    if (!container) {\r\n        console.error(&#96;Container ${tableId} not found&#96;);\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n    \r\n    if (!standingsData) {\r\n        container.innerHTML = &#96;\r\n            \u003cdiv class=\"alert alert-warning\"\u003e\r\n                \u003cp class=\"mb-0\"\u003e${typeof t === 'function' ? t('no_data_available', 'No data available') : 'No data available'}\u003c/p\u003e\r\n            \u003c/div\u003e\r\n        &#96;;\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n    \r\n    // Use Tabulator for structured TableData\r\n    if (typeof createTableTabulator === 'function') {\r\n        console.log(&#96;Creating Tabulator table ${tableId} with ${standingsData.data ? standingsData.data.length : 0} rows&#96;);\r\n        createTableTabulator(tableId, standingsData, { \r\n            disablePositionCircle: false, // Show position circles for league standings\r\n            enableSpecialRowStyling: true,\r\n            tooltips: true,\r\n            disableTeamColorUpdate: true, // Disable automatic team color update (handled manually for multiple tables)\r\n            ...options\r\n        });\r\n    } else if (typeof createTableBootstrap3 === 'function') {\r\n        console.log(&#96;Fallback: Using createTableBootstrap3 for ${tableId}&#96;);\r\n        createTableBootstrap3(tableId, standingsData, {\r\n            disablePositionCircle: false,\r\n            enableSpecialRowStyling: true,\r\n            compact: true,\r\n            ...options\r\n        });\r\n    } else {\r\n        console.error('No table creation function available');\r\n        container.innerHTML = &#96;\r\n            \u003cdiv class=\"alert alert-warning\"\u003e\r\n                \u003cp class=\"mb-0\"\u003e${typeof t === 'function' ? t('no_data_available', 'Table creation function not available') : 'Table creation function not available'}\u003c/p\u003e\r\n            \u003c/div\u003e\r\n        &#96;;\r\n    }\r\n}",

        "originalContents": "\r\n\r\n/**\r\n * Render league standings table\r\n * @param {string} tableId - ID of the container element\r\n * @param {Object} standingsData - TableData object from backend\r\n * @param {Object} options - Additional options\r\n */\r\nfunction renderLeagueStandingsTable(tableId, standingsData, options = {}) {\r\n    const container = document.getElementById(tableId);\r\n    if (!container) {\r\n        console.error(&#96;Container ${tableId} not found&#96;);\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n    \r\n    if (!standingsData) {\r\n        container.innerHTML = &#96;\r\n            \u003cdiv class=\"alert alert-warning\"\u003e\r\n                \u003cp class=\"mb-0\"\u003e${typeof t === 'function' ? t('no_data_available', 'No data available') : 'No data available'}\u003c/p\u003e\r\n            \u003c/div\u003e\r\n        &#96;;\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n    \r\n    // Use Tabulator for structured TableData\r\n    if (typeof createTableTabulator === 'function') {\r\n        console.log(&#96;Creating Tabulator table ${tableId} with ${standingsData.data ? standingsData.data.length : 0} rows&#96;);\r\n        createTableTabulator(tableId, standingsData, { \r\n            disablePositionCircle: false, // Show position circles for league standings\r\n            enableSpecialRowStyling: true,\r\n            tooltips: true,\r\n            disableTeamColorUpdate: true, // Disable automatic team color update (handled manually for multiple tables)\r\n            ...options\r\n        });\r\n    } else if (typeof createTableBootstrap3 === 'function') {\r\n        console.log(&#96;Fallback: Using createTableBootstrap3 for ${tableId}&#96;);\r\n        createTableBootstrap3(tableId, standingsData, {\r\n            disablePositionCircle: false,\r\n            enableSpecialRowStyling: true,\r\n            compact: true,\r\n            ...options\r\n        });\r\n    } else {\r\n        console.error('No table creation function available');\r\n        container.innerHTML = &#96;\r\n            \u003cdiv class=\"alert alert-warning\"\u003e\r\n                \u003cp class=\"mb-0\"\u003e${typeof t === 'function' ? t('no_data_available', 'Table creation function not available') : 'Table creation function not available'}\u003c/p\u003e\r\n            \u003c/div\u003e\r\n        &#96;;\r\n    }\r\n}",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "class Server:\r\n    # fetches basic dataframes from data adapter\r\n    # converts basic dataframes to aggregated dataframes\r\n    # forwards aggregated dataframes to app\n    ...\n    def get_league_week(self, league_name: str, season: str, week: int) -\u003e pd.DataFrame:\n\r\n        ...\n\r\n        data_week_team = self.data_adapter.get_filtered_data(columns=columns, filters=filters_team)\r\n        \r\n        return self.aggregate_league_table(data_week_individual, data_week_team, week)\r\n\r\n    def get_league_standings(self, league_name: str, season: str, week: int) -\u003e pd.DataFrame:\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n        High level database query.\r\n        Coposes a table that represent's a league's standings in a given week, e.g. the sum of all scores and points up until the given week\r\n\r\n        A datafranme for individual results and one for team results are fetched, both dataframes are filtered for league_name, season and weeks.\r\n        Both dataframes contain the columns team_name, points, score and weeks.\r\n        \r\n        The dataframes are aggregated into a single dataframe that contains the columns team_name, points, score, weeks and score_average.\r\n        The score_average is the average score of the individual results as well as the teams' results.\r\n\r\n        The aggregated dataframe is sorted by points and returned\r\n\r\n        Args:\r\n            league_name (str): The name of the league.\r\n            season (str): The season.\r\n            week (int): The week number.\r\n\r\n        Returns:\r\n            pd.DataFrame: The league standings for the given week. Columns: team_name, points, score, week, score_average\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n        filters_individual = {\r\n            Columns.league_name: {'value': league_name, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n            Columns.season: {'value': season, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n            Columns.computed_data: {'value': False, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n            Columns.week: {'value': week+1, 'operator': 'lt'}\r\n        }\r\n        filters_team = {\r\n            Columns.league_name: {'value': league_name, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n            Columns.season: {'value': season, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n            Columns.computed_data: {'value': True, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n            Columns.week: {'value': week+1, 'operator': 'lt'}\r\n        }",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "class Server:\r"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "    # fetches basic dataframes from data adapter\r"
          },

            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "    # converts basic dataframes to aggregated dataframes\r"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "    # forwards aggregated dataframes to app\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12,
            "text": "    "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 243,
            "text": "def get_team_week(self, league_name: str, season: str, team_name: str, week: int) -\u003e pd.DataFrame:\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 267,
            "text": "(columns=columns, filters=filters)\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 268,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 269,
            "text": "    def get_league_week(self, league_name: str, season: str, week: int) -\u003e pd.DataFrame:\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 270,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 305,
            "text": " Columns.score, Columns.week]\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 306,
            "text": "        data_week_individual = self.data_adapter.get_filtered_data(columns=columns, filters=filters_individual)\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 307,
            "text": "        data_week_team = self.data_adapter.get_filtered_data(columns=columns, filters=filters_team)\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 308,
            "text": "        \r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 309,
            "text": "        return self.aggregate_league_table(data_week_individual, data_week_team, week)\r"
          },

          {
            "lineNumber": 311,
            "text": "    def get_league_standings(self, league_name: str, season: str, week: int) -\u003e pd.DataFrame:\r"
          },
          {

            "text": "        \"\"\"\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 313,
            "text": "        High level database query.\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 314,
            "text": "        Coposes a table that represent's a league's standings in a given week, e.g. the sum of all scores and points up until the given week\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 315,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 316,
            "text": "        A datafranme for individual results and one for team results are fetched, both dataframes are filtered for league_name, season and weeks.\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 317,
            "text": "        Both dataframes contain the columns team_name, points, score and weeks.\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 318,
            "text": "        \r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 319,
            "text": "        The dataframes are aggregated into a single dataframe that contains the columns team_name, points, score, weeks and score_average.\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 320,
            "text": "        The score_average is the average score of the individual results as well as the teams' results.\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 321,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 322,
            "text": "        The aggregated dataframe is sorted by points and returned\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 323,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 324,
            "text": "        Args:\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 325,
            "text": "            league_name (str): The name of the league.\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 326,
            "text": "            season (str): The season.\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 327,
            "text": "            week (int): The week number.\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 328,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 329,
            "text": "        Returns:\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 330,
            "text": "            pd.DataFrame: The league standings for the given week. Columns: team_name, points, score, week, score_average\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 331,
            "text": "        \"\"\"\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 332,
            "text": "        filters_individual = {\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 333,
            "text": "            Columns.league_name: {'value': league_name, 'operator': 'eq'},\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 334,
            "text": "            Columns.season: {'value': season, 'operator': 'eq'},\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 335,
            "text": "            Columns.computed_data: {'value': False, 'operator': 'eq'},\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 336,
            "text": "            Columns.week: {'value': week+1, 'operator': 'lt'}\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 337,
            "text": "        }\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 338,
            "text": "        filters_team = {\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 339,
            "text": "            Columns.league_name: {'value': league_name, 'operator': 'eq'},\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 340,
            "text": "            Columns.season: {'value': season, 'operator': 'eq'},\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 341,
            "text": "            Columns.computed_data: {'value': True, 'operator': 'eq'},\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 342,
            "text": "            Columns.week: {'value': week+1, 'operator': 'lt'}\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 343,
            "text": "        }\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 344,
            "text": "        \r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 345,
            "text": "        columns = [Columns.team_name, Columns.points, Columns.score]"
          }
        ],
        "originalContents": "\r\n        data_week_team = self.data_adapter.get_filtered_data(columns=columns, filters=filters_team)\r\n        \r\n        return self.aggregate_league_table(data_week_individual, data_week_team, week)\r\n\r\n    def get_league_standings(self, league_name: str, season: str, week: int) -\u003e pd.DataFrame:\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n        High level database query.\r\n        Coposes a table that represent's a league's standings in a given week, e.g. the sum of all scores and points up until the given week\r\n\r\n        A datafranme for individual results and one for team results are fetched, both dataframes are filtered for league_name, season and weeks.\r\n        Both dataframes contain the columns team_name, points, score and weeks.\r\n        \r\n        The dataframes are aggregated into a single dataframe that contains the columns team_name, points, score, weeks and score_average.\r\n        The score_average is the average score of the individual results as well as the teams' results.\r\n\r\n        The aggregated dataframe is sorted by points and returned\r\n\r\n        Args:\r\n            league_name (str): The name of the league.\r\n            season (str): The season.\r\n            week (int): The week number.\r\n\r\n        Returns:\r\n            pd.DataFrame: The league standings for the given week. Columns: team_name, points, score, week, score_average\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n        filters_individual = {\r\n            Columns.league_name: {'value': league_name, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n            Columns.season: {'value': season, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n            Columns.computed_data: {'value': False, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n            Columns.week: {'value': week+1, 'operator': 'lt'}\r\n        }\r\n        filters_team = {\r\n            Columns.league_name: {'value': league_name, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n            Columns.season: {'value': season, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n            Columns.computed_data: {'value': True, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n            Columns.week: {'value': week+1, 'operator': 'lt'}\r\n        }",

        "contents": "\r\n                // Build CSS classes\r\n                let cssClasses = [];\r\n                if (column.align === 'right') {\r\n                    cssClasses.push('text-end');\r\n                } else if (column.align === 'left') {\r\n                    cssClasses.push('text-start');\r\n                } else {\r\n                    cssClasses.push('text-center');\r\n                }\r\n                \r\n                // Add highlighting class if group is highlighted\r\n                if (isHighlighted) {\r\n                    cssClasses.push('tab-col-highlighted');\r\n                }\r\n                \r\n                columnDefinition.cssClass = cssClasses.join(' ');\r\n\r\n                return columnDefinition;\r\n            }).filter(Boolean)\r\n        };\r\n\r\n        // Set frozen on the parent group, not individual columns\r\n        if (groupFrozenDirection) {\r\n            groupDefinition.frozen = groupFrozenDirection;\r\n        }\r\n\r\n        return groupDefinition;\r\n        }).filter(Boolean);\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    if (!tabulatorColumns.length) {\r\n        console.warn('No Tabulator column definitions could be created');\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    if (window[tableId + 'Instance']) {\r\n        window[tableId + 'Instance'].destroy();\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    const tooltipOption = settings.tooltips\r\n        ? function(cell) {\r\n            const value = cell.getValue();\r\n            if (value === null || value === undefined) {\r\n                return '';\r\n            }\r\n            if (typeof value === 'object') {\r\n                try {\r\n                    return JSON.stringify(value);\r\n                } catch (e) {\r\n                    return '';\r\n                }\r\n            }\r\n            return String(value);\r\n        }\r\n        : false;\r\n\r\n    // Set up initial sort if specified\r\n    const initialSort = data.default_sort ? [\r\n        {\r\n            column: data.default_sort.field,\r\n            dir: data.default_sort.dir || \"desc\"  // Default to descending\r\n        }\r\n    ] : undefined;\r\n",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "function createTableTabulator(tableId, data, options = {}) {\r\n    \r\n    const settings = {\r\n        disablePositionCircle: false,\r\n        enableSpecialRowStyling: false,\r\n        tooltips: true,\r\n        teamField: null,\r\n        enableHeatMap: false,  // Enable heatmap coloring for numeric columns\r\n        ...options\r\n    };\r\n    const tableConfig = data.config || {};\r\n    const isCompactLayout = tableConfig.compact === true;\r\n    const useStripedGroups = tableConfig.stripedColGroups === true;\r\n    // Highlighting style constants\r\n    const HIGHLIGHT_STYLES = {\r\n        headerBackgroundColor: 'rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05)',\r\n        cellBackgroundColor: 'rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.03)',\r\n        headerFontWeight: '900',\r\n        cellFontWeight: '700'\r\n    };\r\n\r\n    if (!data || !data.columns || !data.data) {\r\n        console.error('Invalid data format for Tabulator table');\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    const container = document.getElementById(tableId);\r\n    if (!container) {\r\n        console.error(&#96;Container \"${tableId}\" not found for Tabulator table&#96;);\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n    \r\n    // Ensure container takes full width\r\n    if (container.style.width === '') {\r\n        container.style.width = '100%';\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    const columnOrder = flattenColumnMetadata(data.columns);\r\n    if (!columnOrder.length) {\r\n        console.warn('No column definitions available for Tabulator table');\r\n        const container = document.getElementById(tableId);\r\n        if (container) {\r\n            const errorMsg = data.title \u0026\u0026 data.title.includes('Fehler') ? data.title : 'No column definitions available';\r\n            container.innerHTML = &#96;\u003cdiv class=\"alert alert-warning\"\u003e${errorMsg}\u003c/div\u003e&#96;;\r\n        }\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n    const columnLookup = columnOrder.reduce((acc, info) =\u003e {\r\n        acc[&#96;${info.groupIndex}-${info.columnIndex}&#96;] = info;\r\n        return acc;\r\n    }, {});\r\n",

        "originalContents": "function createTableTabulator(tableId, data, options = {}) {\r\n    \r\n    const settings = {\r\n        disablePositionCircle: false,\r\n        enableSpecialRowStyling: false,\r\n        tooltips: true,\r\n        teamField: null,\r\n        enableHeatMap: false,  // Enable heatmap coloring for numeric columns\r\n        ...options\r\n    };\r\n    const tableConfig = data.config || {};\r\n    const isCompactLayout = tableConfig.compact === true;\r\n    const useStripedGroups = tableConfig.stripedColGroups === true;\r\n    // Highlighting style constants\r\n    const HIGHLIGHT_STYLES = {\r\n        headerBackgroundColor: 'rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05)',\r\n        cellBackgroundColor: 'rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.03)',\r\n        headerFontWeight: '900',\r\n        cellFontWeight: '700'\r\n    };\r\n\r\n    if (!data || !data.columns || !data.data) {\r\n        console.error('Invalid data format for Tabulator table');\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    const container = document.getElementById(tableId);\r\n    if (!container) {\r\n        console.error(&#96;Container \"${tableId}\" not found for Tabulator table&#96;);\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n    \r\n    // Ensure container takes full width\r\n    if (container.style.width === '') {\r\n        container.style.width = '100%';\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    const columnOrder = flattenColumnMetadata(data.columns);\r\n    if (!columnOrder.length) {\r\n        console.warn('No column definitions available for Tabulator table');\r\n        const container = document.getElementById(tableId);\r\n        if (container) {\r\n            const errorMsg = data.title \u0026\u0026 data.title.includes('Fehler') ? data.title : 'No column definitions available';\r\n            container.innerHTML = &#96;\u003cdiv class=\"alert alert-warning\"\u003e${errorMsg}\u003c/div\u003e&#96;;\r\n        }\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n    const columnLookup = columnOrder.reduce((acc, info) =\u003e {\r\n        acc[&#96;${info.groupIndex}-${info.columnIndex}&#96;] = info;\r\n        return acc;\r\n    }, {});\r\n",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\r\n    const tabulatorOptions = {\r\n        data: transformedData,\r\n        columns: tabulatorColumns,\r\n        layout: isCompactLayout ? \"fitData\" : \"fitColumns\",\r\n        responsiveLayout: false,\r\n        pagination: false,\r\n        movableColumns: false,\r\n        tooltips: tooltipOption,\r\n        height: \"auto\",\r\n        rowHeight: 40,\r\n        theme: \"semanticui\",\r\n        cssClass: \"custom-tabulator\",\r\n        columnHeaderVertAlign: \"middle\",\r\n        width: \"100%\", // Ensure table takes full width of container\r\n        ...(initialSort ? { initialSort: initialSort } : {}),\r\n        tableBuilt: function() {\r\n            try {\r\n                // Apply highlighting and fix header separator\r\n                // Use requestAnimationFrame for better timing\r\n                requestAnimationFrame(() =\u003e {\r\n                    requestAnimationFrame(() =\u003e {\r\n                    const tableElement = document.querySelector(&#96;#${tableId}&#96;);\r\n                    if (!tableElement) {\r\n                        return;\r\n                    }\r\n                    \r\n                    // Set CSS custom properties for highlighting styles\r\n                    tableElement.style.setProperty('--highlight-header-bg', HIGHLIGHT_STYLES.headerBackgroundColor);\r\n                    tableElement.style.setProperty('--highlight-cell-bg', HIGHLIGHT_STYLES.cellBackgroundColor);\r\n                    tableElement.style.setProperty('--highlight-header-weight', HIGHLIGHT_STYLES.headerFontWeight);\r\n                    tableElement.style.setProperty('--highlight-cell-weight', HIGHLIGHT_STYLES.cellFontWeight);\r\n                    \r\n                    // Stripe coloring: assign group classes to column group headers\r\n                    // (cells and column headers get classes via cssClass/headerClass in column definitions)\r\n",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1000,
            "text": "window.updateTeamColorMap(teams);\r"
          },

            "lineNumber": 1004,

            "text": "    const tabulatorOptions = {\r"

        ],
        "originalContents": "\r\n    const tabulatorOptions = {\r\n        data: transformedData,\r\n        columns: tabulatorColumns,\r\n        layout: isCompactLayout ? \"fitData\" : \"fitColumns\",\r\n        responsiveLayout: false,\r\n        pagination: false,\r\n        movableColumns: false,\r\n        tooltips: tooltipOption,\r\n        height: \"auto\",\r\n        rowHeight: 40,\r\n        theme: \"semanticui\",\r\n        cssClass: \"custom-tabulator\",\r\n        columnHeaderVertAlign: \"middle\",\r\n        width: \"100%\", // Ensure table takes full width of container\r\n        ...(initialSort ? { initialSort: initialSort } : {}),\r\n        tableBuilt: function() {\r\n            try {\r\n                // Apply highlighting and fix header separator\r\n                // Use requestAnimationFrame for better timing\r\n                requestAnimationFrame(() =\u003e {\r\n                    requestAnimationFrame(() =\u003e {\r\n                    const tableElement = document.querySelector(&#96;#${tableId}&#96;);\r\n                    if (!tableElement) {\r\n                        return;\r\n                    }\r\n                    \r\n                    // Set CSS custom properties for highlighting styles\r\n                    tableElement.style.setProperty('--highlight-header-bg', HIGHLIGHT_STYLES.headerBackgroundColor);\r\n                    tableElement.style.setProperty('--highlight-cell-bg', HIGHLIGHT_STYLES.cellBackgroundColor);\r\n                    tableElement.style.setProperty('--highlight-header-weight', HIGHLIGHT_STYLES.headerFontWeight);\r\n                    tableElement.style.setProperty('--highlight-cell-weight', HIGHLIGHT_STYLES.cellFontWeight);\r\n                    \r\n                    // Stripe coloring: assign group classes to column group headers\r\n                    // (cells and column headers get classes via cssClass/headerClass in column definitions)\r\n",

        "contents": "                        if (sortState[columnId]) {\r\n                            const flatIndex = getFlatColumnIndex(data.columns, groupIndex, index);\r\n                            const direction = sortState[columnId] === 'asc' ? 1 : -1;\r\n                            \r\n                            data.data.sort((a, b) =\u003e {\r\n                                const aVal = a[flatIndex];\r\n                                const bVal = b[flatIndex];\r\n                                \r\n                                // Handle different data types\r\n                                if (typeof aVal === 'number' \u0026\u0026 typeof bVal === 'number') {\r\n                                    return (aVal - bVal) * direction;\r\n                                } else {\r\n                                    const aStr = String(aVal || '');\r\n                                    const bStr = String(bVal || '');\r\n                                    return aStr.localeCompare(bStr) * direction;\r\n                                }\r\n                            });\r\n                        }\r\n                        \r\n                        // Update the table body\r\n                        updateTableBody(data.data, enableSpecialRowStyling);\r\n                    });\r\n                } else {\r\n                    th.textContent = col.title || '';\r\n                    // Add tooltip to header: use explicit tooltip if provided, otherwise default to title\r\n                    const tooltipText = (col.tooltip !== undefined \u0026\u0026 col.tooltip !== null) \r\n                        ? col.tooltip \r\n                        : col.title || '';\r\n                    if (tooltipText) {\r\n                        th.setAttribute('title', tooltipText);\r\n                    }\r\n                }\r\n                \r\n                // Add vertical borders between groups\r\n",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\u003clink href=\"{{ static_url('vendor/tabulator/css/tabulator_semanticui.min.css') }}\" rel=\"stylesheet\"\u003e\r\n\u003cscript src=\"{{ static_url('js/theme/color-utils.js') }}\"\u003e\u003c/script\u003e\r\n\u003cscript type=\"text/javascript\" src=\"{{ static_url('vendor/tabulator/js/tabulator.min.js') }}\"\u003e\u003c/script\u003e\r\n\r\n\u003cscript\u003e\r\nfunction createTable(config) {\r\n    const {\r\n        data,\r\n        columns,\r\n        headerGroups = [],  // This contains the actual colspan values\r\n        rowNumbering = true,\r\n        positionChange = false\r\n    } = config;\r\n\r\n    let currentData = [...data];\r\n\r\n    const colors = ColorUtils.getGradientColors(\r\n        headerGroups.length, \r\n        ColorUtils.getThemeColor('surface') || '#ffffff', \r\n        ColorUtils.getThemeColor('accent') || '#7ec2f8'\r\n    );\r\n\r\n    // Create mappings for column colors and last group columns\r\n    const columnColors = {};\r\n    const lastGroupColumns = {};\r\n    let currentColumn = 0;\r\n    headerGroups.forEach((group, groupIndex) =\u003e {\r\n        const isLastGroup = groupIndex === headerGroups.length - 1;\r\n        const colspan = group.colspan || 1;  // Get actual colspan from the group\r\n        for (let i = 0; i \u003c colspan; i++) {\r\n            columnColors[currentColumn] = colors[groupIndex];\r\n            lastGroupColumns[currentColumn] = isLastGroup;\r\n            currentColumn++;\r\n        }\r\n    });\r\n\r\n    const createHeaderGroups = () =\u003e {\r\n        if (!headerGroups || headerGroups.length === 0) return '';\r\n        \r\n        return &#96;\r\n            \u003ctr\u003e\r\n                ${headerGroups.map((group, index) =\u003e &#96;\r\n                    \u003cth class=\"text-center\" \r\n                        colspan=\"${group.colspan || 1}\" \r\n                        style=\"background-color: ${colors[index]}; \r\n                               color: ${index === headerGroups.length - 1 ? 'white' : 'black'};\"\u003e\r\n                        ${group.title || ''}\r\n                    \u003c/th\u003e\r\n                &#96;).join('')}\r\n            \u003c/tr\u003e\r\n        &#96;;\r\n    };\r\n",

        "originalContents": "\u003clink href=\"{{ static_url('vendor/tabulator/css/tabulator_semanticui.min.css') }}\" rel=\"stylesheet\"\u003e\r\n\u003cscript src=\"{{ static_url('js/theme/color-utils.js') }}\"\u003e\u003c/script\u003e\r\n\u003cscript type=\"text/javascript\" src=\"{{ static_url('vendor/tabulator/js/tabulator.min.js') }}\"\u003e\u003c/script\u003e\r\n\r\n\u003cscript\u003e\r\nfunction createTable(config) {\r\n    const {\r\n        data,\r\n        columns,\r\n        headerGroups = [],  // This contains the actual colspan values\r\n        rowNumbering = true,\r\n        positionChange = false\r\n    } = config;\r\n\r\n    let currentData = [...data];\r\n\r\n    const colors = ColorUtils.getGradientColors(\r\n        headerGroups.length, \r\n        ColorUtils.getThemeColor('surface') || '#ffffff', \r\n        ColorUtils.getThemeColor('accent') || '#7ec2f8'\r\n    );\r\n\r\n    // Create mappings for column colors and last group columns\r\n    const columnColors = {};\r\n    const lastGroupColumns = {};\r\n    let currentColumn = 0;\r\n    headerGroups.forEach((group, groupIndex) =\u003e {\r\n        const isLastGroup = groupIndex === headerGroups.length - 1;\r\n        const colspan = group.colspan || 1;  // Get actual colspan from the group\r\n        for (let i = 0; i \u003c colspan; i++) {\r\n            columnColors[currentColumn] = colors[groupIndex];\r\n            lastGroupColumns[currentColumn] = isLastGroup;\r\n            currentColumn++;\r\n        }\r\n    });\r\n\r\n    const createHeaderGroups = () =\u003e {\r\n        if (!headerGroups || headerGroups.length === 0) return '';\r\n        \r\n        return &#96;\r\n            \u003ctr\u003e\r\n                ${headerGroups.map((group, index) =\u003e &#96;\r\n                    \u003cth class=\"text-center\" \r\n                        colspan=\"${group.colspan || 1}\" \r\n                        style=\"background-color: ${colors[index]}; \r\n                               color: ${index === headerGroups.length - 1 ? 'white' : 'black'};\"\u003e\r\n                        ${group.title || ''}\r\n                    \u003c/th\u003e\r\n                &#96;).join('')}\r\n            \u003c/tr\u003e\r\n        &#96;;\r\n    };\r\n",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\r\n\r\n@dataclass\r\nclass TableData:\r\n    \"\"\"Complete table data structure.\"\"\"\r\n    columns: Union[List[ColumnGroup], List[Column]]  # Accept either ColumnGroups or bare Columns\r\n    data: List[List[Any]]\r\n    title: Optional[str] = None\r\n    description: Optional[str] = None\r\n    config: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = field(default_factory=dict)  # Table-wide configuration\r\n    metadata: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = field(default_factory=dict)  # Additional metadata for i18n and UI\r\n    row_metadata: Optional[List[Dict[str, Any]]] = field(default_factory=list)  # Row-level metadata for styling\r\n    cell_metadata: Optional[Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]]] = field(default_factory=dict)  # Cell-level metadata for styling (format: \"row:col\")\r\n    default_sort: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None  # Default sort: {\"field\": \"average\", \"dir\": \"desc\"}\r\n    \r\n    def __post_init__(self):\r\n        \"\"\"Normalize columns: if bare Columns are provided, wrap them in a ColumnGroup with empty title.\"\"\"\r\n        if self.columns and len(self.columns) \u003e 0 and isinstance(self.columns[0], Column):\r\n            # Convert List[Column] to List[ColumnGroup] by wrapping in a single group\r\n            self.columns = [ColumnGroup(title=\"\", columns=list(self.columns))]",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 19,
            "text": "@dataclass\r"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "class ColumnGroup:\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27,
            "text": "  # Width for the entire group\r"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "    highlighted: bool = False  # Whether this group should be visually highlighted\r"
          },

        "originalContents": "\r\n\r\n@dataclass\r\nclass TableData:\r\n    \"\"\"Complete table data structure.\"\"\"\r\n    columns: Union[List[ColumnGroup], List[Column]]  # Accept either ColumnGroups or bare Columns\r\n    data: List[List[Any]]\r\n    title: Optional[str] = None\r\n    description: Optional[str] = None\r\n    config: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = field(default_factory=dict)  # Table-wide configuration\r\n    metadata: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = field(default_factory=dict)  # Additional metadata for i18n and UI\r\n    row_metadata: Optional[List[Dict[str, Any]]] = field(default_factory=list)  # Row-level metadata for styling\r\n    cell_metadata: Optional[Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]]] = field(default_factory=dict)  # Cell-level metadata for styling (format: \"row:col\")\r\n    default_sort: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None  # Default sort: {\"field\": \"average\", \"dir\": \"desc\"}\r\n    \r\n    def __post_init__(self):\r\n        \"\"\"Normalize columns: if bare Columns are provided, wrap them in a ColumnGroup with empty title.\"\"\"\r\n        if self.columns and len(self.columns) \u003e 0 and isinstance(self.columns[0], Column):\r\n            # Convert List[Column] to List[ColumnGroup] by wrapping in a single group\r\n            self.columns = [ColumnGroup(title=\"\", columns=list(self.columns))]",
        "range": {

Status: ${response.status}&#96;);\r\n                error.status = response.status;\r\n                throw error;\r\n            }\r\n            return response.json();\r\n        })\r\n        .then(data =\u003e {\r\n            // Check if data indicates no results\r\n            if (data.message \u0026\u0026 data.message.includes('No data found')) {\r\n                throw new Error('No data found for these parameters');\r\n            }\r\n            // Render the table with the fetched data - use Tabulator by default\r\n            if (typeof createTableTabulator === 'function') {\r\n                createTableTabulator(tableId, data, {\r\n                    ...params.options,\r\n                    tooltips: true\r\n                });\r\n            } else if (typeof createTableBootstrap3 === 'function') {\r\n                // Fallback to Bootstrap if Tabulator not available\r\n                createTableBootstrap3(tableId, data, params.options || {});\r\n            } else {\r\n                console.error('No table creation function available');\r\n                container.innerHTML = '\u003cdiv class=\"alert alert-warning\"\u003eTable creation function not available\u003c/div\u003e';\r\n            }\r\n        })\r\n",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\r\n\r\n/**\r\n * Render league standings table\r\n * @param {string} tableId - ID of the container element\r\n * @param {Object} standingsData - TableData object from backend\r\n * @param {Object} options - Additional options\r\n */\r\nfunction renderLeagueStandingsTable(tableId, standingsData, options = {}) {\r\n    const container = document.getElementById(tableId);\r\n    if (!container) {\r\n        console.error(&#96;Container ${tableId} not found&#96;);\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n    \r\n    if (!standingsData) {\r\n        container.innerHTML = &#96;\r\n            \u003cdiv class=\"alert alert-warning\"\u003e\r\n                \u003cp class=\"mb-0\"\u003e${typeof t === 'function' ? t('no_data_available', 'No data available') : 'No data available'}\u003c/p\u003e\r\n            \u003c/div\u003e\r\n        &#96;;\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n    \r\n    // Use Tabulator for structured TableData\r\n    if (typeof createTableTabulator === 'function') {\r\n        console.log(&#96;Creating Tabulator table ${tableId} with ${standingsData.data ? standingsData.data.length : 0} rows&#96;);\r\n        createTableTabulator(tableId, standingsData, { \r\n            disablePositionCircle: false, // Show position circles for league standings\r\n            enableSpecialRowStyling: true,\r\n            tooltips: true,\r\n            disableTeamColorUpdate: true, // Disable automatic team color update (handled manually for multiple tables)\r\n            ...options\r\n        });\r\n    } else if (typeof createTableBootstrap3 === 'function') {\r\n        console.log(&#96;Fallback: Using createTableBootstrap3 for ${tableId}&#96;);\r\n        createTableBootstrap3(tableId, standingsData, {\r\n            disablePositionCircle: false,\r\n            enableSpecialRowStyling: true,\r\n            compact: true,\r\n            ...options\r\n        });\r\n    } else {\r\n        console.error('No table creation function available');\r\n        container.innerHTML = &#96;\r\n            \u003cdiv class=\"alert alert-warning\"\u003e\r\n                \u003cp class=\"mb-0\"\u003e${typeof t === 'function' ? t('no_data_available', 'Table creation function not available') : 'Table creation function not available'}\u003c/p\u003e\r\n            \u003c/div\u003e\r\n        &#96;;\r\n    }\r\n}",
        "detailedLines": [
          {

            "text": "                \u003cp class=\"mb-0\"\u003e${typeof t === 'function' ? t('no_data_available', 'Table creation function not available') : 'Table creation function not available'}\u003c/p\u003e\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 120,
            "text": "            \u003c/div\u003e\r"
          },
          {

            "text": "        &#96;;\r"

            "lineNumber": 122,

          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 123,
            "text": "}\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 124,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 125,
            "text": "/**\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 126,
            "text": " * Populate honor scores sections\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 127,
            "text": " * @param {Object} honorScoresData - Honor scores data from backend\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 128,
            "text": " * @param {string} prefix - Prefix for element IDs (optional, for multiple leagues)\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 129,
            "text": " */"
          }
        ],
        "originalContents": "\r\n\r\n/**\r\n * Render league standings table\r\n * @param {string} tableId - ID of the container element\r\n * @param {Object} standingsData - TableData object from backend\r\n * @param {Object} options - Additional options\r\n */\r\nfunction renderLeagueStandingsTable(tableId, standingsData, options = {}) {\r\n    const container = document.getElementById(tableId);\r\n    if (!container) {\r\n        console.error(&#96;Container ${tableId} not found&#96;);\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n    \r\n    if (!standingsData) {\r\n        container.innerHTML = &#96;\r\n            \u003cdiv class=\"alert alert-warning\"\u003e\r\n                \u003cp class=\"mb-0\"\u003e${typeof t === 'function' ? t('no_data_available', 'No data available') : 'No data available'}\u003c/p\u003e\r\n            \u003c/div\u003e\r\n        &#96;;\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n    \r\n    // Use Tabulator for structured TableData\r\n    if (typeof createTableTabulator === 'function') {\r\n        console.log(&#96;Creating Tabulator table ${tableId} with ${standingsData.data ? standingsData.data.length : 0} rows&#96;);\r\n        createTableTabulator(tableId, standingsData, { \r\n            disablePositionCircle: false, // Show position circles for league standings\r\n            enableSpecialRowStyling: true,\r\n            tooltips: true,\r\n            disableTeamColorUpdate: true, // Disable automatic team color update (handled manually for multiple tables)\r\n            ...options\r\n        });\r\n    } else if (typeof createTableBootstrap3 === 'function') {\r\n        console.log(&#96;Fallback: Using createTableBootstrap3 for ${tableId}&#96;);\r\n        createTableBootstrap3(tableId, standingsData, {\r\n            disablePositionCircle: false,\r\n            enableSpecialRowStyling: true,\r\n            compact: true,\r\n            ...options\r\n        });\r\n    } else {\r\n        console.error('No table creation function available');\r\n        container.innerHTML = &#96;\r\n            \u003cdiv class=\"alert alert-warning\"\u003e\r\n                \u003cp class=\"mb-0\"\u003e${typeof t === 'function' ? t('no_data_available', 'Table creation function not available') : 'Table creation function not available'}\u003c/p\u003e\r\n            \u003c/div\u003e\r\n        &#96;;\r\n    }\r\n}",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "column": 3,
            "line": 128
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 15
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "app/static/js/utils/league-standings-card-utils.js"
      },

        "contents": "                if (col.sortable !== false) {\r\n                    th.style.cursor = 'pointer';\r\n                    \r\n                    // Create header content with sort indicator\r\n                    const headerContent = document.createElement('div');\r\n                    headerContent.style.display = 'flex';\r\n                    headerContent.style.alignItems = 'center';\r\n                    headerContent.style.justifyContent = col.align === 'left' ? 'flex-start' : \r\n                                                        col.align === 'right' ? 'flex-end' : 'center';\r\n                    headerContent.style.gap = '8px';\r\n                    \r\n                    const titleSpan = document.createElement('span');\r\n                    titleSpan.textContent = col.title || '';\r\n                    // Add tooltip to header: use explicit tooltip if provided, otherwise default to title\r\n                    const tooltipText = (col.tooltip !== undefined \u0026\u0026 col.tooltip !== null) \r\n                        ? col.tooltip \r\n                        : col.title || '';\r\n                    if (tooltipText) {\r\n                        th.setAttribute('title', tooltipText);\r\n                    }\r\n                    headerContent.appendChild(titleSpan);\r\n                    \r\n                    const sortIcon = document.createElement('span');\r\n                    sortIcon.style.width = '12px';\r\n                    sortIcon.style.height = '12px';\r\n                    sortIcon.style.display = 'inline-block';\r\n                    sortIcon.style.opacity = '0.5';\r\n                    sortIcon.innerHTML = '↕';  // Default sort icon\r\n                    headerContent.appendChild(sortIcon);\r\n                    \r\n                    th.appendChild(headerContent);\r\n                    \r\n                    // Add sort functionality\r\n                    const columnId = &#96;${groupIndex}-${index}&#96;;\r\n",

    {

        "contents": "function renderTeamVsTeamComparison(data, options) {\n...\n\u003c/span\u003e\r\n                                            \u003cdiv class=\"heat-map-legend\" style=\"background: ${window.ColorUtils?.getThemeColor('heatMapHigh') || '#1b8da7'};         "contents": "class GameOverviewBlock extends BaseContentBlock {\n    ...\n    async loadGameOverviewData(state) {\n\r\n            ...\n\r\n            if (container) {\r\n                // Use Tabulator for the game overview table\r\n                if (typeof createTableTabulator === 'function') {\r\n                    console.log('Using createTableTabulator function for game overview table');\r\n                    createTableTabulator('tableGameOverview', tableData, { \r\n                        disablePositionCircle: false, // Enable position circles for team and opponent positions\r\n                        enableSpecialRowStyling: true,\r\n                        tooltips: true,\r\n                        enableHeatMap: true // Enable heatmap coloring for pins and points columns\r\n                    });\r\n                } else if (typeof createTableBootstrap3 === 'function') {\r\n                    console.log('Fallback: Using createTableBootstrap3 function');\r\n                    createTableBootstrap3('tableGameOverview', tableData, { \r\n                        disablePositionCircle: true,\r\n                        enableSpecialRowStyling: true \r\n                    });\r\n                } else {\r\n                    console.error('No table creation function available');\r\n                    container.innerHTML = &#96;\u003cdiv class=\"alert alert-warning\"\u003e${typeof t === 'function' ? t('no_data', 'Table creation function not available') : 'Table creation function not available'}\u003c/div\u003e&#96;;\r\n                }\r\n            }\r\n        } catch (error) {\r\n            console.error('Error loading game overview:', error);\r\n            const container = document.getElementById('tableGameOverview');\r\n            if (container) {\r\n                container.innerHTML = &#96;\u003cdiv class=\"alert alert-danger\"\u003e${typeof t === 'function' ? t('error_loading_data', 'Error loading game overview') : 'Error loading game overview'}: ${error.message || error}\u003c/div\u003e&#96;;\r\n            }\r\n        }\r\n    }\r\n}",

        "originalContents": "\r\n            if (container) {\r\n                // Use Tabulator for the game overview table\r\n                if (typeof createTableTabulator === 'function') {\r\n                    console.log('Using createTableTabulator function for game overview table');\r\n                    createTableTabulator('tableGameOverview', tableData, { \r\n                        disablePositionCircle: false, // Enable position circles for team and opponent positions\r\n                        enableSpecialRowStyling: true,\r\n                        tooltips: true,\r\n                        enableHeatMap: true // Enable heatmap coloring for pins and points columns\r\n                    });\r\n                } else if (typeof createTableBootstrap3 === 'function') {\r\n                    console.log('Fallback: Using createTableBootstrap3 function');\r\n                    createTableBootstrap3('tableGameOverview', tableData, { \r\n                        disablePositionCircle: true,\r\n                        enableSpecialRowStyling: true \r\n                    });\r\n                } else {\r\n                    console.error('No table creation function available');\r\n                    container.innerHTML = &#96;\u003cdiv class=\"alert alert-warning\"\u003e${typeof t === 'function' ? t('no_data', 'Table creation function not available') : 'Table creation function not available'}\u003c/div\u003e&#96;;\r\n                }\r\n            }\r\n        } catch (error) {\r\n            console.error('Error loading game overview:', error);\r\n            const container = document.getElementById('tableGameOverview');\r\n            if (container) {\r\n                container.innerHTML = &#96;\u003cdiv class=\"alert alert-danger\"\u003e${typeof t === 'function' ? t('error_loading_data', 'Error loading game overview') : 'Error loading game overview'}: ${error.message || error}\u003c/div\u003e&#96;;\r\n            }\r\n        }\r\n    }\r\n}",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "column": 1,
            "line": 174
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "column": 3,
            "line": 3
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "app/static/js/content-blocks/game-overview-block.js"
      },
      "score": 0.28818583488464355
    }
  ]
}
```

        "contents": "class MatchDayBlock extends BaseContentBlock {\n\r\n\r\n    ...\n\r\n\r\n    async loadLeagueWeekTable(season, league, week) {\r\n        try {\r\n            const response = await fetchWithDatabase(&#96;/league/get_league_week_table?season=${season}\u0026league=${league}\u0026week=${week}&#96;);\r\n            const tableData = await response.json();\r\n            \r\n            console.log('League week table data:', tableData); // Debug logging\r\n            \r\n            // Use shared utility function\r\n            if (typeof window.renderLeagueStandingsTable === 'function') {\r\n                window.renderLeagueStandingsTable('tableLeagueWeek', tableData);\r\n            } else {\r\n                // Fallback to old implementation\r\n                const container = document.getElementById('tableLeagueWeek');\r\n                if (container) {\r\n                    if (typeof createTableTabulator === 'function') {\r\n                        createTableTabulator('tableLeagueWeek', tableData, { \r\n                            disablePositionCircle: false,\r\n                            enableSpecialRowStyling: true,\r\n                            tooltips: true\r\n                        });\r\n                    } else if (typeof createTableBootstrap3 === 'function') {\r\n                        createTableBootstrap3('tableLeagueWeek', tableData, { \r\n                            disablePositionCircle: false,\r\n                            enableSpecialRowStyling: true \r\n                        });\r\n                    } else {\r\n                        container.innerHTML = &#96;\u003cdiv class=\"alert alert-warning\"\u003e${typeof t === 'function' ? t('no_data', 'Table creation function not available') : 'Table creation function not available'}\u003c/div\u003e&#96;;\r\n                    }\r\n                }\r\n            }\r\n        }",

        "originalContents": "\r\n\r\n    async loadLeagueWeekTable(season, league, week) {\r\n        try {\r\n            const response = await fetchWithDatabase(&#96;/league/get_league_week_table?season=${season}\u0026league=${league}\u0026week=${week}&#96;);\r\n            const tableData = await response.json();\r\n            \r\n            console.log('League week table data:', tableData); // Debug logging\r\n            \r\n            // Use shared utility function\r\n            if (typeof window.renderLeagueStandingsTable === 'function') {\r\n                window.renderLeagueStandingsTable('tableLeagueWeek', tableData);\r\n            } else {\r\n                // Fallback to old implementation\r\n                const container = document.getElementById('tableLeagueWeek');\r\n                if (container) {\r\n                    if (typeof createTableTabulator === 'function') {\r\n                        createTableTabulator('tableLeagueWeek', tableData, { \r\n                            disablePositionCircle: false,\r\n                            enableSpecialRowStyling: true,\r\n                            tooltips: true\r\n                        });\r\n                    } else if (typeof createTableBootstrap3 === 'function') {\r\n                        createTableBootstrap3('tableLeagueWeek', tableData, { \r\n                            disablePositionCircle: false,\r\n                            enableSpecialRowStyling: true \r\n                        });\r\n                    } else {\r\n                        container.innerHTML = &#96;\u003cdiv class=\"alert alert-warning\"\u003e${typeof t === 'function' ? t('no_data', 'Table creation function not available') : 'Table creation function not available'}\u003c/div\u003e&#96;;\r\n                    }\r\n                }\r\n            }\r\n        }",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\r\n\r\n/**\r\n * Update league week standings table\r\n */\r\nfunction updateTableLeagueWeek() {\r\n    const selectedSeason = currentState?.season || document.querySelector('input[name=\"season\"]:checked')?.value;\r\n    const selectedLeague = currentState?.league || document.querySelector('input[name=\"league\"]:checked')?.value;\r\n    const selectedWeek = currentState?.week || document.querySelector('input[name=\"week\"]:checked')?.value;\r\n    \r\n    if (!selectedSeason || !selectedLeague) {\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n    \r\n    if (!selectedWeek) {\r\n        // Show message when no week is selected\r\n        const messageArea = document.getElementById('weekMessage');\r\n        const standingsTable = document.getElementById('tableLeagueWeek');\r\n        \r\n        if (messageArea) {\r\n            messageArea.style.display = 'block';\r\n            messageArea.innerHTML = window.translations ? \r\n                (window.translations['please_select_match_day'] || 'Please select a match day.') : \r\n                'Please select a match day.';\r\n        }\r\n        if (standingsTable) standingsTable.style.display = 'none';\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n    \r\n    // Show the table and hide the message\r\n    const messageArea = document.getElementById('weekMessage');\r\n    const standingsTable = document.getElementById('tableLeagueWeek');\r\n    \r\n    if (messageArea) messageArea.style.display = 'none';\r\n    if (standingsTable) standingsTable.style.display = 'block';\r\n    \r\n    // Fetch and display table\r\n    fetchAndRenderTable('tableLeagueWeek', '/league/get_league_week_table', {\r\n        season: selectedSeason,\r\n        league: selectedLeague,\r\n        week: selectedWeek\r\n    });\r\n}",

        "originalContents": "\r\n\r\n/**\r\n * Update league week standings table\r\n */\r\nfunction updateTableLeagueWeek() {\r\n    const selectedSeason = currentState?.season || document.querySelector('input[name=\"season\"]:checked')?.value;\r\n    const selectedLeague = currentState?.league || document.querySelector('input[name=\"league\"]:checked')?.value;\r\n    const selectedWeek = currentState?.week || document.querySelector('input[name=\"week\"]:checked')?.value;\r\n    \r\n    if (!selectedSeason || !selectedLeague) {\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n    \r\n    if (!selectedWeek) {\r\n        // Show message when no week is selected\r\n        const messageArea = document.getElementById('weekMessage');\r\n        const standingsTable = document.getElementById('tableLeagueWeek');\r\n        \r\n        if (messageArea) {\r\n            messageArea.style.display = 'block';\r\n            messageArea.innerHTML = window.translations ? \r\n                (window.translations['please_select_match_day'] || 'Please select a match day.') : \r\n                'Please select a match day.';\r\n        }\r\n        if (standingsTable) standingsTable.style.display = 'none';\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n    \r\n    // Show the table and hide the message\r\n    const messageArea = document.getElementById('weekMessage');\r\n    const standingsTable = document.getElementById('tableLeagueWeek');\r\n    \r\n    if (messageArea) messageArea.style.display = 'none';\r\n    if (standingsTable) standingsTable.style.display = 'block';\r\n    \r\n    // Fetch and display table\r\n    fetchAndRenderTable('tableLeagueWeek', '/league/get_league_week_table', {\r\n        season: selectedSeason,\r\n        league: selectedLeague,\r\n        week: selectedWeek\r\n    });\r\n}",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "column": 1,
            "line": 181
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 61
          }
