        "relativeWorkspacePath": "app/static/js/league/league-data-utils.js"
      },
      "score": 0.4284310042858124
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\r\n\r\n/**\r\n * Render league standings table\r\n * @param {string} tableId - ID of the container element\r\n * @param {Object} standingsData - TableData object from backend\r\n * @param {Object} options - Additional options\r\n */\r\nfunction renderLeagueStandingsTable(tableId, standingsData, options = {}) {\r\n    const container = document.getElementById(tableId);\r\n    if (!container) {\r\n        console.error(&#96;Container ${tableId} not found&#96;);\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n    \r\n    if (!standingsData) {\r\n        container.innerHTML = &#96;\r\n            \u003cdiv class=\"alert alert-warning\"\u003e\r\n                \u003cp class=\"mb-0\"\u003e${typeof t === 'function' ? t('no_data_available', 'No data available') : 'No data available'}\u003c/p\u003e\r\n            \u003c/div\u003e\r\n        &#96;;\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n    \r\n    // Use Tabulator for structured TableData\r\n    if (typeof createTableTabulator === 'function') {\r\n        console.log(&#96;Creating Tabulator table ${tableId} with ${standingsData.data ? standingsData.data.length : 0} rows&#96;);\r\n        createTableTabulator(tableId, standingsData, { \r\n            disablePositionCircle: false, // Show position circles for league standings\r\n            enableSpecialRowStyling: true,\r\n            tooltips: true,\r\n            disableTeamColorUpdate: true, // Disable automatic team color update (handled manually for multiple tables)\r\n            ...options\r\n        });\r\n    } else if (typeof createTableBootstrap3 === 'function') {\r\n        console.log(&#96;Fallback: Using createTableBootstrap3 for ${tableId}&#96;);\r\n        createTableBootstrap3(tableId, standingsData, {\r\n            disablePositionCircle: false,\r\n            enableSpecialRowStyling: true,\r\n            compact: true,\r\n            ...options\r\n        });\r\n    } else {\r\n        console.error('No table creation function available');\r\n        container.innerHTML = &#96;\r\n            \u003cdiv class=\"alert alert-warning\"\u003e\r\n                \u003cp class=\"mb-0\"\u003e${typeof t === 'function' ? t('no_data_available', 'Table creation function not available') : 'Table creation function not available'}\u003c/p\u003e\r\n            \u003c/div\u003e\r\n        &#96;;\r\n    }\r\n}",
        "detailedLines": [

          },

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 125,
            "text": "/**\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 126,
            "text": " * Populate honor scores sections\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 127,
            "text": " * @param {Object} honorScoresData - Honor scores data from backend\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 128,
            "text": " * @param {string} prefix - Prefix for element IDs (optional, for multiple leagues)\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 129,
            "text": " */"
          }
        ],
        "originalContents": "\r\n\r\n/**\r\n * Render league standings table\r\n * @param {string} tableId - ID of the container element\r\n * @param {Object} standingsData - TableData object from backend\r\n * @param {Object} options - Additional options\r\n */\r\nfunction renderLeagueStandingsTable(tableId, standingsData, options = {}) {\r\n    const container = document.getElementById(tableId);\r\n    if (!container) {\r\n        console.error(&#96;Container ${tableId} not found&#96;);\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n    \r\n    if (!standingsData) {\r\n        container.innerHTML = &#96;\r\n            \u003cdiv class=\"alert alert-warning\"\u003e\r\n                \u003cp class=\"mb-0\"\u003e${typeof t === 'function' ? t('no_data_available', 'No data available') : 'No data available'}\u003c/p\u003e\r\n            \u003c/div\u003e\r\n        &#96;;\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n    \r\n    // Use Tabulator for structured TableData\r\n    if (typeof createTableTabulator === 'function') {\r\n        console.log(&#96;Creating Tabulator table ${tableId} with ${standingsData.data ? standingsData.data.length : 0} rows&#96;);\r\n        createTableTabulator(tableId, standingsData, { \r\n            disablePositionCircle: false, // Show position circles for league standings\r\n            enableSpecialRowStyling: true,\r\n            tooltips: true,\r\n            disableTeamColorUpdate: true, // Disable automatic team color update (handled manually for multiple tables)\r\n            ...options\r\n        });\r\n    } else if (typeof createTableBootstrap3 === 'function') {\r\n        console.log(&#96;Fallback: Using createTableBootstrap3 for ${tableId}&#96;);\r\n        createTableBootstrap3(tableId, standingsData, {\r\n            disablePositionCircle: false,\r\n            enableSpecialRowStyling: true,\r\n            compact: true,\r\n            ...options\r\n        });\r\n    } else {\r\n        console.error('No table creation function available');\r\n        container.innerHTML = &#96;\r\n            \u003cdiv class=\"alert alert-warning\"\u003e\r\n                \u003cp class=\"mb-0\"\u003e${typeof t === 'function' ? t('no_data_available', 'Table creation function not available') : 'Table creation function not available'}\u003c/p\u003e\r\n            \u003c/div\u003e\r\n        &#96;;\r\n    }\r\n}",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "column": 3,
            "line": 128
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 15
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "app/static/js/utils/league-standings-card-utils.js"

    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "/**\r\n * League Data Utilities\r\n * \r\n * Data fetching and display functions for league statistics\r\n * Extracted from monolithic league stats template\r\n */\r\n\r\n/**\r\n * Update team details table\r\n */\r\nfunction updateTableTeamDetails() {\r\n    const selectedSeason = currentState?.season || document.querySelector('input[name=\"season\"]:checked')?.value;\r\n    const selectedLeague = currentState?.league || document.querySelector('input[name=\"league\"]:checked')?.value;\r\n    const selectedWeek = currentState?.week || document.querySelector('input[name=\"week\"]:checked')?.value;\r\n    const selectedTeam = currentState?.team || document.querySelector('input[name=\"team\"]:checked')?.value;\r\n    const selectedView = currentState?.teamView || document.querySelector('input[name=\"teamView\"]:checked')?.value;\r\n    \r\n    let endpoint = '/league/get_team_week_details_table';\r\n    let viewMode = undefined;\r\n    \r\n    if (selectedView === 'new') {\r\n        endpoint = '/league/get_team_individual_scores_table';\r\n    } else if (selectedView === 'newFull') {\r\n        endpoint = '/league/get_team_week_head_to_head_table';\r\n        viewMode = 'full';\r\n    }\r\n    \r\n    if (!selectedSeason || !selectedLeague || !selectedWeek || !selectedTeam) {\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n    \r\n    // Update header\r\n    const matchDayText = window.translations ? (window.translations['match_day_label'] || 'Match Day') : 'Match Day';\r\n    const headerElement = document.getElementById('teamDetailsMetadata');\r\n    if (headerElement) {\r\n        headerElement.innerHTML = &#96;${selectedTeam} - ${matchDayText} ${selectedWeek}&#96;;\r\n    }\r\n    \r\n    // Use fetchAndRenderTable for consistent table rendering\r\n    const params = {\r\n        season: selectedSeason,\r\n        league: selectedLeague,\r\n        week: selectedWeek,\r\n        team: selectedTeam,\r\n        options: {\r\n            disablePositionCircle: true,\r\n            mergeCells: false,\r\n            enableSpecialRowStyling: true\r\n        }\r\n    };\r\n    \r\n    if (viewMode) {\r\n        params.view_mode = viewMode;\r\n    }\r\n    \r\n    fetchAndRenderTable('teamTableWeekDetails', endpoint, params);\r\n}",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "/**\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2,
            "text": " * League Data Utilities\r"

        ],
        "originalContents": "/**\r\n * League Data Utilities\r\n * \r\n * Data fetching and display functions for league statistics\r\n * Extracted from monolithic league stats template\r\n */\r\n\r\n/**\r\n * Update team details table\r\n */\r\nfunction updateTableTeamDetails() {\r\n    const selectedSeason = currentState?.season || document.querySelector('input[name=\"season\"]:checked')?.value;\r\n    const selectedLeague = currentState?.league || document.querySelector('input[name=\"league\"]:checked')?.value;\r\n    const selectedWeek = currentState?.week || document.querySelector('input[name=\"week\"]:checked')?.value;\r\n    const selectedTeam = currentState?.team || document.querySelector('input[name=\"team\"]:checked')?.value;\r\n    const selectedView = currentState?.teamView || document.querySelector('input[name=\"teamView\"]:checked')?.value;\r\n    \r\n    let endpoint = '/league/get_team_week_details_table';\r\n    let viewMode = undefined;\r\n    \r\n    if (selectedView === 'new') {\r\n        endpoint = '/league/get_team_individual_scores_table';\r\n    } else if (selectedView === 'newFull') {\r\n        endpoint = '/league/get_team_week_head_to_head_table';\r\n        viewMode = 'full';\r\n    }\r\n    \r\n    if (!selectedSeason || !selectedLeague || !selectedWeek || !selectedTeam) {\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n    \r\n    // Update header\r\n    const matchDayText = window.translations ? (window.translations['match_day_label'] || 'Match Day') : 'Match Day';\r\n    const headerElement = document.getElementById('teamDetailsMetadata');\r\n    if (headerElement) {\r\n        headerElement.innerHTML = &#96;${selectedTeam} - ${matchDayText} ${selectedWeek}&#96;;\r\n    }\r\n    \r\n    // Use fetchAndRenderTable for consistent table rendering\r\n    const params = {\r\n        season: selectedSeason,\r\n        league: selectedLeague,\r\n        week: selectedWeek,\r\n        team: selectedTeam,\r\n        options: {\r\n            disablePositionCircle: true,\r\n            mergeCells: false,\r\n            enableSpecialRowStyling: true\r\n        }\r\n    };\r\n    \r\n    if (viewMode) {\r\n        params.view_mode = viewMode;\r\n    }\r\n    \r\n    fetchAndRenderTable('teamTableWeekDetails', endpoint, params);\r\n}",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "class LeagueService:\n    ...\n    def get_game_team_details_data(self, season: str, league: str, week: int, team: str, round_number: int) -\u003e TableData:\n\n            \n        ...\n\n            \n        except Exception as e:\n            print(f\"Error in get_game_team_details_data: {e}\")\n            import traceback\n            traceback.print_exc()\n            return TableData(\n                columns=[],\n                data=[],\n                title=f\"Error loading game team details data\"\n            )\n\n    def get_league_standings(self, season: str, league: str, week: Optional[int] = None) -\u003e LeagueStandings:\n        \"\"\"\n        Get league standings for a specific season, league, and week.\n        \n        Args:\n            season: The season identifier\n            league: The league name\n            week: The week number (if None, gets the latest week)\n            \n        Returns:\n            LeagueStandings object with team performances\n        \"\"\"\n        # If week is not specified, get the latest week\n        if week is None:\n            week = self.get_latest_week(season, league)\n        \n        print(\"week: \", week)\n\n        # Get league statistics\n        stats = self.stats_service.get_league_statistics(league, season)\n        if not stats:\n            return LeagueStandings(\n                season=season,\n                league_name=league,\n                week=week,\n                teams=[],\n                last_updated=datetime.datetime.now().isoformat()\n            )\n        \n        # Convert statistics to team performances\n        team_performances = []",

          }
        ],
        "originalContents": "\n            \n        except Exception as e:\n            print(f\"Error in get_game_team_details_data: {e}\")\n            import traceback\n            traceback.print_exc()\n            return TableData(\n                columns=[],\n                data=[],\n                title=f\"Error loading game team details data\"\n            )\n\n    def get_league_standings(self, season: str, league: str, week: Optional[int] = None) -\u003e LeagueStandings:\n        \"\"\"\n        Get league standings for a specific season, league, and week.\n        \n        Args:\n            season: The season identifier\n            league: The league name\n            week: The week number (if None, gets the latest week)\n            \n        Returns:\n            LeagueStandings object with team performances\n        \"\"\"\n        # If week is not specified, get the latest week\n        if week is None:\n            week = self.get_latest_week(season, league)\n        \n        print(\"week: \", week)\n\n        # Get league statistics\n        stats = self.stats_service.get_league_statistics(league, season)\n        if not stats:\n            return LeagueStandings(\n                season=season,\n                league_name=league,\n                week=week,\n                teams=[],\n                last_updated=datetime.datetime.now().isoformat()\n            )\n        \n        # Convert statistics to team performances\n        team_performances = []",

          }

{\n            // Load existing endpoints first, handle missing ones gracefully\n            const dataPromises = [];\n            \n            // Always try to load league standings (existing endpoint)\n            dataPromises.push(\n                this.loadLeagueStandings(league, season).catch(error =\u003e {\n                    console.warn('Could not load league standings:', error);\n                    return null;\n                })\n            );\n            \n            // Always try to load team points (existing endpoint)\n            dataPromises.push(\n                this.loadTeamPoints(league, season).catch(error =\u003e {\n                    console.warn('Could not load team points:', error);\n                    return null;\n                })\n            );\n            \n            // Always try to load team positions (existing endpoint)\n            dataPromises.push(\n                this.loadTeamPositions(league, season).catch(error =\u003e {\n                    console.warn('Could not load team positions:', error);\n                    return null;\n                })\n            );\n            \n            // Try optional endpoints with fallbacks\n            dataPromises.push(\n                this.loadTimetable(league, season).catch(error =\u003e {\n                    console.warn('Timetable endpoint not available, creating placeholder');\n                    return this.createMockTimetable(season);\n                })\n            );\n            \n            const [standingsData, pointsData, positionsData, timetableData] = await Promise.all(dataPromises);\n\n            // Create components with available data\n            if (timetableData) this.createTimetable(timetableData);\n            if (standingsData) this.createStandingsTable(standingsData);",

        "originalContents": "\n\n    async loadSeasonData(state) {\n        const { league, season } = state;\n        \n        try {\n            // Load existing endpoints first, handle missing ones gracefully\n            const dataPromises = [];\n            \n            // Always try to load league standings (existing endpoint)\n            dataPromises.push(\n                this.loadLeagueStandings(league, season).catch(error =\u003e {\n                    console.warn('Could not load league standings:', error);\n                    return null;\n                })\n            );\n            \n            // Always try to load team points (existing endpoint)\n            dataPromises.push(\n                this.loadTeamPoints(league, season).catch(error =\u003e {\n                    console.warn('Could not load team points:', error);\n                    return null;\n                })\n            );\n            \n            // Always try to load team positions (existing endpoint)\n            dataPromises.push(\n                this.loadTeamPositions(league, season).catch(error =\u003e {\n                    console.warn('Could not load team positions:', error);\n                    return null;\n                })\n            );\n            \n            // Try optional endpoints with fallbacks\n            dataPromises.push(\n                this.loadTimetable(league, season).catch(error =\u003e {\n                    console.warn('Timetable endpoint not available, creating placeholder');\n                    return this.createMockTimetable(season);\n                })\n            );\n            \n            const [standingsData, pointsData, positionsData, timetableData] = await Promise.all(dataPromises);\n\n            // Create components with available data\n            if (timetableData) this.createTimetable(timetableData);\n            if (standingsData) this.createStandingsTable(standingsData);",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "class SeasonOverviewBlock extends BaseContentBlock {\n\n\n    ...\n\n\n    async loadLeagueHistoryTable(season, league) {\n        try {\n            const response = await fetchWithDatabase(&#96;/league/get_league_history?season=${season}\u0026league=${league}&#96;);\n            const tableData = await response.json();\n            \n            console.log('League history table data:', tableData); // Debug logging\n            \n            const container = document.getElementById('leagueTableHistory');\n            if (container) {\n                // Use the proper createTableBootstrap3 function for structured TableData\n                if (typeof createTableBootstrap3 === 'function') {\n                    console.log('Using createTableBootstrap3 function for TableData');\n                    createTableBootstrap3('leagueTableHistory', tableData, { \n                        disablePositionCircle: false, // League history should show team color circles\n                        enableSpecialRowStyling: true \n                    });\n                } else if (typeof createTable === 'function') {\n                    console.log('Fallback: Using createTable function');\n                    const tableHTML = createTable(tableData);\n                    container.innerHTML = tableHTML;\n                } else {\n                    console.error('No table creation function available');\n                    container.innerHTML = '\u003cdiv class=\"alert alert-warning\"\u003eTable creation function not available\u003c/div\u003e';\n                }\n            }\n        } catch (error) {\n            console.error('Error loading league history table:', error);\n            const container = document.getElementById('leagueTableHistory');\n            if (container) {\n                container.innerHTML = '\u003cdiv class=\"alert alert-danger\"\u003eError loading league table\u003c/div\u003e';\n            }\n        }\n    }",
        "detailedLines": [
          {

          },
          {

            "text": "    async loadPositionChart(season, league) {"
          },

            "lineNumber": 225,
            "text": "        try"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 260,
            "text": "    }"
          }
        ],
        "originalContents": "\n\n    async loadLeagueHistoryTable(season, league) {\n        try {\n            const response = await fetchWithDatabase(&#96;/league/get_league_history?season=${season}\u0026league=${league}&#96;);\n            const tableData = await response.json();\n            \n            console.log('League history table data:', tableData); // Debug logging\n            \n            const container = document.getElementById('leagueTableHistory');\n            if (container) {\n                // Use the proper createTableBootstrap3 function for structured TableData\n                if (typeof createTableBootstrap3 === 'function') {\n                    console.log('Using createTableBootstrap3 function for TableData');\n                    createTableBootstrap3('leagueTableHistory', tableData, { \n                        disablePositionCircle: false, // League history should show team color circles\n                        enableSpecialRowStyling: true \n                    });\n                } else if (typeof createTable === 'function') {\n                    console.log('Fallback: Using createTable function');\n                    const tableHTML = createTable(tableData);\n                    container.innerHTML = tableHTML;\n                } else {\n                    console.error('No table creation function available');\n                    container.innerHTML = '\u003cdiv class=\"alert alert-warning\"\u003eTable creation function not available\u003c/div\u003e';\n                }\n            }\n        } catch (error) {\n            console.error('Error loading league history table:', error);\n            const container = document.getElementById('leagueTableHistory');\n            if (container) {\n                container.innerHTML = '\u003cdiv class=\"alert alert-danger\"\u003eError loading league table\u003c/div\u003e';\n            }\n        }\n    }",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "column": 1,
            "line": 360
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "column": 3,
            "line": 3
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "app/static/js/content-blocks/season-overview-block.js"
      },
      "score": 0.35643890500068665
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "class Server:\r\n    # fetches basic dataframes from data adapter\r\n    # converts basic dataframes to aggregated dataframes\r\n    # forwards aggregated dataframes to app\n    ...\n    def get_league_week(self, league_name: str, season: str, week: int) -\u003e pd.DataFrame:\n\r\n        ...\n\r\n        data_week_team = self.data_adapter.get_filtered_data(columns=columns, filters=filters_team)\r\n        \r\n        return self.aggregate_league_table(data_week_individual, data_week_team, week)\r\n\r\n    def get_league_standings(self, league_name: str, season: str, week: int) -\u003e pd.DataFrame:\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n        High level database query.\r\n        Coposes a table that represent's a league's standings in a given week, e.g. the sum of all scores and points up until the given week\r\n\r\n        A datafranme for individual results and one for team results are fetched, both dataframes are filtered for league_name, season and weeks.\r\n        Both dataframes contain the columns team_name, points, score and weeks.\r\n        \r\n        The dataframes are aggregated into a single dataframe that contains the columns team_name, points, score, weeks and score_average.\r\n        The score_average is the average score of the individual results as well as the teams' results.\r\n\r\n        The aggregated dataframe is sorted by points and returned\r\n\r\n        Args:\r\n            league_name (str): The name of the league.\r\n            season (str): The season.\r\n            week (int): The week number.\r\n\r\n        Returns:\r\n            pd.DataFrame: The league standings for the given week. Columns: team_name, points, score, week, score_average\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n        filters_individual = {\r\n            Columns.league_name: {'value': league_name, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n            Columns.season: {'value': season, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n            Columns.computed_data: {'value': False, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n            Columns.week: {'value': week+1, 'operator': 'lt'}\r\n        }\r\n        filters_team = {\r\n            Columns.league_name: {'value': league_name, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n            Columns.season: {'value': season, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n            Columns.computed_data: {'value': True, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n            Columns.week: {'value': week+1, 'operator': 'lt'}\r\n        }",

              # Sort teams: first by season_points (descending), then by season_score (descending) as tiebreaker
              # When points are equal, team with higher score (pins) should rank higher
+             # Using tuple sorting: Python compares tuples element by element
+             # With reverse=True, larger values come first, so (74, 13839) &gt; (74, 13809)
              sorted_teams = sorted(
                  teams,
                  key=lambda t: (
-                     -float(team_data[t]['season_points'] or 0),  # Negative for descending
-                     -float(team_data[t]['season_score'] or 0)     # Negative for descending tiebreaker
-                 )

+                     float(team_data[t]['season_score'] or 0)

_**Agent (composer-1)**_


Added debug logging to verify the sorting. The logic should work: with `reverse=True`, when points are equal, teams with more pins should rank higher.

The debug output will show teams with the same points and their scores to confirm the tiebreaker. Please check the console/logs when loading the table to see if the sorting is applied correctly.

If the issue persists, Tabulator may be re-sorting the table. We can:
1. Disable Tabulator sorting on the position column
2. Set an explicit initial sort to preserve backend order

Should I implement one of these, or do you want to check the debug output first?

<details><summary>Tool use: **search_replace** • Edit file: app/services/league_service.py</summary>

                  for team in sorted_teams:
-                     points = team_data[team]['season_points']
-                     if points not in points_groups:
-                         points_groups[points] = []
-                     points_groups[points].append({
+                     points_raw = team_data[team]['season_points']
+                     points_rounded = round(points_raw, 1)  # Round to 1 decimal for grouping

                          'team': team,
-                         'points': points,

-                         print(f"DEBUG: Teams with {points} points: {[(g['team'], g['score']) for g in group]}")

_**Agent (composer-1)**_


Fixed the syntax error: nested f-strings aren't allowed in Python. Updated the code to use `round()` instead of nested f-string formatting.

The debug code should now work. Reload the page and check the console output. It will show:
1. The first 10 teams with their points and scores
2. Any teams that have the same rounded points value
3. The expected order when ties are detected

This should help identify why the tiebreaker sorting isn't working as expected.

---

_**Agent (composer-1)**_


Removed all debug code and cleaned up the comments. The sorting logic remains: teams are sorted by `season_points` (descending), and when points are equal, by `season_score` (descending) as the tiebreaker.

The issue was setting decimals to 0, which caused floating-point precision problems. With the correct decimal settings, the sorting should work correctly.

        "contents": "\r\n                // Adjust column widths based on group\r\n                // First group (Rang/Position) should be wider, week/total groups should be compressed\r\n                let columnWidth = parseColumnWidth(column.width);\r\n                \r\n                if (columnWidth === undefined) {\r\n                    // Apply default widths based on group\r\n                    const groupTitle = (group.title || '').toLowerCase();\r\n                    const isWeekGroup = groupTitle.includes('week') || groupTitle.includes('woche');\r\n                    const isTotalGroup = groupTitle.includes('total') || groupTitle.includes('gesamt') || groupTitle.includes('summe');\r\n                    \r\n                    if (groupIndex === 0) {\r\n                        // First group (Rang) - make it wider\r\n                        columnWidth = 120;\r\n                    } else if (isWeekGroup || isTotalGroup) {\r\n                        // Week and Total groups - compress them\r\n                        columnWidth = 80;\r\n                    } else {\r\n                        // Default width for other groups\r\n                        columnWidth = 100;\r\n                    }\r\n                }\r\n\r\n                if (columnWidth !== undefined) {\r\n                    if (isCompactLayout) {\r\n                        columnDefinition.width = columnWidth;\r\n                        columnDefinition.minWidth = columnWidth;\r\n                        columnDefinition.widthGrow = 0;\r\n                    } else {\r\n                        columnDefinition.minWidth = columnWidth; // Set minimum width, allow expansion\r\n                        columnDefinition.widthGrow = 1; // Allow column to grow proportionally\r\n                    }\r\n                } else if (isCompactLayout) {\r\n                    columnDefinition.widthGrow = 0;\r\n                }\r\n",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "function createTableTabulator(tableId, data, options = {}) {\r\n    \r\n    const settings = {\r\n        disablePositionCircle: false,\r\n        enableSpecialRowStyling: false,\r\n        tooltips: true,\r\n        teamField: null,\r\n        enableHeatMap: false,  // Enable heatmap coloring for numeric columns\r\n        ...options\r\n    };\r\n    const tableConfig = data.config || {};\r\n    const isCompactLayout = tableConfig.compact === true;\r\n    const useStripedGroups = tableConfig.stripedColGroups === true;\r\n    // Highlighting style constants\r\n    const HIGHLIGHT_STYLES = {\r\n        headerBackgroundColor: 'rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05)',\r\n        cellBackgroundColor: 'rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.03)',\r\n        headerFontWeight: '900',\r\n        cellFontWeight: '700'\r\n    };\r\n\r\n    if (!data || !data.columns || !data.data) {\r\n        console.error('Invalid data format for Tabulator table');\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    const container = document.getElementById(tableId);\r\n    if (!container) {\r\n        console.error(&#96;Container \"${tableId}\" not found for Tabulator table&#96;);\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n    \r\n    // Ensure container takes full width\r\n    if (container.style.width === '') {\r\n        container.style.width = '100%';\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    const columnOrder = flattenColumnMetadata(data.columns);\r\n    if (!columnOrder.length) {\r\n        console.warn('No column definitions available for Tabulator table');\r\n        const container = document.getElementById(tableId);\r\n        if (container) {\r\n            const errorMsg = data.title \u0026\u0026 data.title.includes('Fehler') ? data.title : 'No column definitions available';\r\n            container.innerHTML = &#96;\u003cdiv class=\"alert alert-warning\"\u003e${errorMsg}\u003c/div\u003e&#96;;\r\n        }\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n    const columnLookup = columnOrder.reduce((acc, info) =\u003e {\r\n        acc[&#96;${info.groupIndex}-${info.columnIndex}&#96;] = info;\r\n        return acc;\r\n    }, {});\r\n",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 159,
            "text": "    return Number.isNaN(parsed) ? undefined : parsed;\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 160,
            "text": "}\r"
          },

            "lineNumber": 161,
            "text": "\r"
          },

            "text": "    const settings = {\r"

            "lineNumber": 171,
            "text": "    };\r"

          {
            "lineNumber": 172,
            "text": "    const tableConfig = data.config || {};\r"
          },

            "lineNumber": 173,
            "text": "    const isCompactLayout = tableConfig.compact === true;\r"

          {
            "lineNumber": 174,
            "text": "    const useStripedGroups = tableConfig.stripedColGroups === true;\r"
          },

        "originalContents": "function createTableTabulator(tableId, data, options = {}) {\r\n    \r\n    const settings = {\r\n        disablePositionCircle: false,\r\n        enableSpecialRowStyling: false,\r\n        tooltips: true,\r\n        teamField: null,\r\n        enableHeatMap: false,  // Enable heatmap coloring for numeric columns\r\n        ...options\r\n    };\r\n    const tableConfig = data.config || {};\r\n    const isCompactLayout = tableConfig.compact === true;\r\n    const useStripedGroups = tableConfig.stripedColGroups === true;\r\n    // Highlighting style constants\r\n    const HIGHLIGHT_STYLES = {\r\n        headerBackgroundColor: 'rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05)',\r\n        cellBackgroundColor: 'rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.03)',\r\n        headerFontWeight: '900',\r\n        cellFontWeight: '700'\r\n    };\r\n\r\n    if (!data || !data.columns || !data.data) {\r\n        console.error('Invalid data format for Tabulator table');\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    const container = document.getElementById(tableId);\r\n    if (!container) {\r\n        console.error(&#96;Container \"${tableId}\" not found for Tabulator table&#96;);\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n    \r\n    // Ensure container takes full width\r\n    if (container.style.width === '') {\r\n        container.style.width = '100%';\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    const columnOrder = flattenColumnMetadata(data.columns);\r\n    if (!columnOrder.length) {\r\n        console.warn('No column definitions available for Tabulator table');\r\n        const container = document.getElementById(tableId);\r\n        if (container) {\r\n            const errorMsg = data.title \u0026\u0026 data.title.includes('Fehler') ? data.title : 'No column definitions available';\r\n            container.innerHTML = &#96;\u003cdiv class=\"alert alert-warning\"\u003e${errorMsg}\u003c/div\u003e&#96;;\r\n        }\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n    const columnLookup = columnOrder.reduce((acc, info) =\u003e {\r\n        acc[&#96;${info.groupIndex}-${info.columnIndex}&#96;] = info;\r\n        return acc;\r\n    }, {});\r\n",
        "range": {

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\r\n    const tabulatorOptions = {\r\n        data: transformedData,\r\n        columns: tabulatorColumns,\r\n        layout: isCompactLayout ? \"fitData\" : \"fitColumns\",\r\n        responsiveLayout: false,\r\n        pagination: false,\r\n        movableColumns: false,\r\n        tooltips: tooltipOption,\r\n        height: \"auto\",\r\n        rowHeight: 40,\r\n        theme: \"semanticui\",\r\n        cssClass: \"custom-tabulator\",\r\n        columnHeaderVertAlign: \"middle\",\r\n        width: \"100%\", // Ensure table takes full width of container\r\n        ...(initialSort ? { initialSort: initialSort } : {}),\r\n        tableBuilt: function() {\r\n            try {\r\n                // Apply highlighting and fix header separator\r\n                // Use requestAnimationFrame for better timing\r\n                requestAnimationFrame(() =\u003e {\r\n                    requestAnimationFrame(() =\u003e {\r\n                    const tableElement = document.querySelector(&#96;#${tableId}&#96;);\r\n                    if (!tableElement) {\r\n                        return;\r\n                    }\r\n                    \r\n                    // Set CSS custom properties for highlighting styles\r\n                    tableElement.style.setProperty('--highlight-header-bg', HIGHLIGHT_STYLES.headerBackgroundColor);\r\n                    tableElement.style.setProperty('--highlight-cell-bg', HIGHLIGHT_STYLES.cellBackgroundColor);\r\n                    tableElement.style.setProperty('--highlight-header-weight', HIGHLIGHT_STYLES.headerFontWeight);\r\n                    tableElement.style.setProperty('--highlight-cell-weight', HIGHLIGHT_STYLES.cellFontWeight);\r\n                    \r\n                    // Stripe coloring: assign group classes to column group headers\r\n                    // (cells and column headers get classes via cssClass/headerClass in column definitions)\r\n",

        "originalContents": "\r\n    const tabulatorOptions = {\r\n        data: transformedData,\r\n        columns: tabulatorColumns,\r\n        layout: isCompactLayout ? \"fitData\" : \"fitColumns\",\r\n        responsiveLayout: false,\r\n        pagination: false,\r\n        movableColumns: false,\r\n        tooltips: tooltipOption,\r\n        height: \"auto\",\r\n        rowHeight: 40,\r\n        theme: \"semanticui\",\r\n        cssClass: \"custom-tabulator\",\r\n        columnHeaderVertAlign: \"middle\",\r\n        width: \"100%\", // Ensure table takes full width of container\r\n        ...(initialSort ? { initialSort: initialSort } : {}),\r\n        tableBuilt: function() {\r\n            try {\r\n                // Apply highlighting and fix header separator\r\n                // Use requestAnimationFrame for better timing\r\n                requestAnimationFrame(() =\u003e {\r\n                    requestAnimationFrame(() =\u003e {\r\n                    const tableElement = document.querySelector(&#96;#${tableId}&#96;);\r\n                    if (!tableElement) {\r\n                        return;\r\n                    }\r\n                    \r\n                    // Set CSS custom properties for highlighting styles\r\n                    tableElement.style.setProperty('--highlight-header-bg', HIGHLIGHT_STYLES.headerBackgroundColor);\r\n                    tableElement.style.setProperty('--highlight-cell-bg', HIGHLIGHT_STYLES.cellBackgroundColor);\r\n                    tableElement.style.setProperty('--highlight-header-weight', HIGHLIGHT_STYLES.headerFontWeight);\r\n                    tableElement.style.setProperty('--highlight-cell-weight', HIGHLIGHT_STYLES.cellFontWeight);\r\n                    \r\n                    // Stripe coloring: assign group classes to column group headers\r\n                    // (cells and column headers get classes via cssClass/headerClass in column definitions)\r\n",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "function flattenColumnMetadata(columnGroups = []) {\r\n    const flatColumns = [];\r\n    columnGroups.forEach((group, groupIndex) =\u003e {\r\n        if (Array.isArray(group.columns)) {\r\n            group.columns.forEach((column, columnIndex) =\u003e {\r\n                const field = column.field || &#96;col_${groupIndex}_${columnIndex}&#96;;\r\n                flatColumns.push({\r\n                    group,\r\n                    column,\r\n                    groupIndex,\r\n                    columnIndex,\r\n                    field\r\n                });\r\n            });\r\n        }\r\n    });\r\n    return flatColumns;\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction mapCellMetadata(cellMetadata = {}, columnOrder = []) {\r\n    const mapped = {};\r\n    Object.entries(cellMetadata).forEach(([key, styles]) =\u003e {\r\n        const [rowIndexStr, colIndexStr] = key.split(':');\r\n        const rowIndex = parseInt(rowIndexStr, 10);\r\n        const colIndex = parseInt(colIndexStr, 10);\r\n        const columnInfo = columnOrder[colIndex];\r\n        if (!columnInfo || Number.isNaN(rowIndex)) {\r\n            return;\r\n        }\r\n        const field = columnInfo.field;\r\n        if (!mapped[rowIndex]) {\r\n            mapped[rowIndex] = {};\r\n        }\r\n        mapped[rowIndex][field] = styles;\r\n    });\r\n    return mapped;\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction applyElementStyles(element, styles = {}) {\r\n    if (!element || !styles) {\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n    Object.entries(styles).forEach(([property, value]) =\u003e {\r\n        if (value !== undefined \u0026\u0026 value !== null) {\r\n            element.style[property] = value;\r\n        }\r\n    });\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction parseColumnWidth(width) {\r\n    if (!width) {\r\n        return undefined;\r\n    }\r\n    if (typeof width === 'number') {\r\n        return width;\r\n    }\r\n    const parsed = parseInt(width, 10);\r\n    return Number.isNaN(parsed) ? undefined : parsed;\r\n}\r\n\r\n",
        "detailedLines": [
          {

          }
        ],
        "originalContents": "function flattenColumnMetadata(columnGroups = []) {\r\n    const flatColumns = [];\r\n    columnGroups.forEach((group, groupIndex) =\u003e {\r\n        if (Array.isArray(group.columns)) {\r\n            group.columns.forEach((column, columnIndex) =\u003e {\r\n                const field = column.field || &#96;col_${groupIndex}_${columnIndex}&#96;;\r\n                flatColumns.push({\r\n                    group,\r\n                    column,\r\n                    groupIndex,\r\n                    columnIndex,\r\n                    field\r\n                });\r\n            });\r\n        }\r\n    });\r\n    return flatColumns;\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction mapCellMetadata(cellMetadata = {}, columnOrder = []) {\r\n    const mapped = {};\r\n    Object.entries(cellMetadata).forEach(([key, styles]) =\u003e {\r\n        const [rowIndexStr, colIndexStr] = key.split(':');\r\n        const rowIndex = parseInt(rowIndexStr, 10);\r\n        const colIndex = parseInt(colIndexStr, 10);\r\n        const columnInfo = columnOrder[colIndex];\r\n        if (!columnInfo || Number.isNaN(rowIndex)) {\r\n            return;\r\n        }\r\n        const field = columnInfo.field;\r\n        if (!mapped[rowIndex]) {\r\n            mapped[rowIndex] = {};\r\n        }\r\n        mapped[rowIndex][field] = styles;\r\n    });\r\n    return mapped;\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction applyElementStyles(element, styles = {}) {\r\n    if (!element || !styles) {\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n    Object.entries(styles).forEach(([property, value]) =\u003e {\r\n        if (value !== undefined \u0026\u0026 value !== null) {\r\n            element.style[property] = value;\r\n        }\r\n    });\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction parseColumnWidth(width) {\r\n    if (!width) {\r\n        return undefined;\r\n    }\r\n    if (typeof width === 'number') {\r\n        return width;\r\n    }\r\n    const parsed = parseInt(width, 10);\r\n    return Number.isNaN(parsed) ? undefined : parsed;\r\n}\r\n\r\n",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 163
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "column": 12,
            "line": 95
          }

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\r\n                let colCssClass = column.cssClass || '';\r\n                let colHeaderClass = column.headerClass || '';\r\n                \r\n                // Build CSS classes\r\n                let cssClasses = [];\r\n                if (column.align === 'right') {\r\n                    cssClasses.push('text-end');\r\n                } else if (column.align === 'left') {\r\n                    cssClasses.push('text-start');\r\n                } else {\r\n                    cssClasses.push('text-center');\r\n                }\r\n                \r\n                if (isHighlighted) {\r\n                    cssClasses.push('tab-col-highlighted');\r\n                }\r\n                \r\n                if (colCssClass) {\r\n                    cssClasses.push(colCssClass);\r\n                }\r\n\r\n                // Determine if we should use custom formatter or Tabulator's built-in number formatter\r\n                let formatterToUse = formatter;\r\n                let formatterParamsToUse = {};\r\n                \r\n                // If decimal_places is specified, we MUST use our custom formatter\r\n                // (Tabulator's number formatter doesn't support custom decimal places)\r\n                if (decimalPlaces !== null \u0026\u0026 !isNaN(decimalPlaces) \u0026\u0026 decimalPlaces \u003e= 0) {\r\n                    formatterToUse = formatter;\r\n                    formatterParamsToUse = {};\r\n                    // Debug: Log that we're using custom formatter\r\n                    if (field.startsWith('week_')) {\r\n                        console.log(&#96;[Formatter Setup] Field: \"${field}\", Using custom formatter with decimalPlaces: ${decimalPlaces}&#96;);\r\n                    }\r\n                }\r\n                \r\n",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\r\n                // Build CSS classes\r\n                let cssClasses = [];\r\n                if (column.align === 'right') {\r\n                    cssClasses.push('text-end');\r\n                } else if (column.align === 'left') {\r\n                    cssClasses.push('text-start');\r\n                } else {\r\n                    cssClasses.push('text-center');\r\n                }\r\n                \r\n                // Add highlighting class if group is highlighted\r\n                if (isHighlighted) {\r\n                    cssClasses.push('tab-col-highlighted');\r\n                }\r\n                \r\n                columnDefinition.cssClass = cssClasses.join(' ');\r\n\r\n                return columnDefinition;\r\n            }).filter(Boolean)\r\n        };\r\n\r\n        // Set frozen on the parent group, not individual columns\r\n        if (groupFrozenDirection) {\r\n            groupDefinition.frozen = groupFrozenDirection;\r\n        }\r\n\r\n        return groupDefinition;\r\n        }).filter(Boolean);\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    if (!tabulatorColumns.length) {\r\n        console.warn('No Tabulator column definitions could be created');\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    if (window[tableId + 'Instance']) {\r\n        window[tableId + 'Instance'].destroy();\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    const tooltipOption = settings.tooltips\r\n        ? function(cell) {\r\n            const value = cell.getValue();\r\n            if (value === null || value === undefined) {\r\n                return '';\r\n            }\r\n            if (typeof value === 'object') {\r\n                try {\r\n                    return JSON.stringify(value);\r\n                } catch (e) {\r\n                    return '';\r\n                }\r\n            }\r\n            return String(value);\r\n        }\r\n        : false;\r\n\r\n    // Set up initial sort if specified\r\n    const initialSort = data.default_sort ? [\r\n        {\r\n            column: data.default_sort.field,\r\n            dir: data.default_sort.dir || \"desc\"  // Default to descending\r\n        }\r\n    ] : undefined;\r\n",

        "originalContents": "\r\n                // Build CSS classes\r\n                let cssClasses = [];\r\n                if (column.align === 'right') {\r\n                    cssClasses.push('text-end');\r\n                } else if (column.align === 'left') {\r\n                    cssClasses.push('text-start');\r\n                } else {\r\n                    cssClasses.push('text-center');\r\n                }\r\n                \r\n                // Add highlighting class if group is highlighted\r\n                if (isHighlighted) {\r\n                    cssClasses.push('tab-col-highlighted');\r\n                }\r\n                \r\n                columnDefinition.cssClass = cssClasses.join(' ');\r\n\r\n                return columnDefinition;\r\n            }).filter(Boolean)\r\n        };\r\n\r\n        // Set frozen on the parent group, not individual columns\r\n        if (groupFrozenDirection) {\r\n            groupDefinition.frozen = groupFrozenDirection;\r\n        }\r\n\r\n        return groupDefinition;\r\n        }).filter(Boolean);\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    if (!tabulatorColumns.length) {\r\n        console.warn('No Tabulator column definitions could be created');\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    if (window[tableId + 'Instance']) {\r\n        window[tableId + 'Instance'].destroy();\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    const tooltipOption = settings.tooltips\r\n        ? function(cell) {\r\n            const value = cell.getValue();\r\n            if (value === null || value === undefined) {\r\n                return '';\r\n            }\r\n            if (typeof value === 'object') {\r\n                try {\r\n                    return JSON.stringify(value);\r\n                } catch (e) {\r\n                    return '';\r\n                }\r\n            }\r\n            return String(value);\r\n        }\r\n        : false;\r\n\r\n    // Set up initial sort if specified\r\n    const initialSort = data.default_sort ? [\r\n        {\r\n            column: data.default_sort.field,\r\n            dir: data.default_sort.dir || \"desc\"  // Default to descending\r\n        }\r\n    ] : undefined;\r\n",

        "contents": "\r\n    // If no group titles, flatten to direct columns (no groups)\r\n    let tabulatorColumns;\r\n    if (!hasGroupTitles) {\r\n        // Flatten: extract all columns from all groups and pass directly to Tabulator\r\n        tabulatorColumns = [];\r\n        data.columns.forEach((group, groupIndex) =\u003e {\r\n            if (!Array.isArray(group.columns)) {\r\n                return;\r\n            }\r\n            \r\n            const groupFrozenDirection = group.frozen === 'left' || group.frozen === 'right' ? group.frozen : null;\r\n            const isHighlighted = group.highlighted === true;\r\n            \r\n            group.columns.forEach((column, columnIndex) =\u003e {\r\n                const columnInfo = columnLookup[&#96;${groupIndex}-${columnIndex}&#96;];\r\n                if (!columnInfo) {\r\n                    return;\r\n                }\r\n                const field = columnInfo.field;\r\n                const headerAlign = column.align || 'center';\r\n                const columnStyles = column.style || {};\r\n                // Store decimal_places in a variable to ensure it's captured correctly in the closure\r\n                // Check both column.decimal_places and columnInfo.column.decimal_places for compatibility\r\n                let decimalPlaces = null;\r\n                if (column.decimal_places !== undefined \u0026\u0026 column.decimal_places !== null) {\r\n                    decimalPlaces = parseInt(column.decimal_places);\r\n                } else if (columnInfo.column \u0026\u0026 columnInfo.column.decimal_places !== undefined \u0026\u0026 columnInfo.column.decimal_places !== null) {\r\n                    decimalPlaces = parseInt(columnInfo.column.decimal_places);\r\n                }\r\n                \r\n                // Debug: Log ALL columns to see structure (filter in console if needed)\r\n                // ALWAYS log for week columns to verify code is running\r\n",

(const group of data.columns) {\r\n                            if (group.columns) {\r\n                                for (const col of group.columns) {\r\n                                    if (col.field === field) {\r\n                                        if (col.tooltip !== undefined \u0026\u0026 col.tooltip !== null) {\r\n                                            tooltipText = col.tooltip;\r\n                                        }\r\n                                        break;\r\n                                    }\r\n                                }\r\n                            }\r\n                        }\r\n                    }\r\n                    \r\n                    // Set tooltip on both the column element and title element\r\n                    colElement.setAttribute('title', tooltipText);\r\n                    titleElement.setAttribute('title', tooltipText);\r\n                });\r\n            });\r\n        } catch (error) {\r\n            console.error(&#96;[Tabulator ${tableId}] Error in tableBuilt event handler:&#96;, error);\r\n        }\r\n    });\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction createTableBootstrap3(tableId, data, options = {}) {\r\n    // Check if the data has columns and data\r\n    if (!data || !data.columns || !data.data) {\r\n        console.error('Invalid data format');\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    // Normalize columns: if bare Column objects are provided (have 'field' but no 'columns'),\r\n    // wrap them in a ColumnGroup with empty title to match expected structure\r\n    if (data.columns.length \u003e 0 \u0026\u0026 data.columns[0].field \u0026\u0026 !data.columns[0].columns) {\r\n        // These are bare Column objects, wrap them in a single group\r\n        data.columns = [{\r\n            title: \"\",\r\n            columns: data.columns\r\n        }];\r\n    }\r\n",

_**Agent (composer-1)**_


Made two changes:

1. **Changed the layout mode**: When `compact == False`, switched from `"fitColumns"` to `"fitDataStretch"`. This fits columns to their content first, then stretches them proportionally to fill available space.

2. **Adjusted `widthGrow` values** based on content type:
   - **Team/player/name columns**: `widthGrow = 3` (grow 3x more)
   - **Other text columns**: `widthGrow = 2` (grow 2x more)
   - **Numeric columns**: `widthGrow = 1` (grow normally)

When tables expand in non-compact mode, columns with more content (like team names) will get more space, while numeric columns get proportionally less. The `fitDataStretch` layout ensures columns fit their content first, then distribute extra space based on the `widthGrow` ratios.

---

_**Agent (composer-1)**_


Updated the layout to use `fitDataFill` with equal `widthGrow` values (all set to 1). This should:
1. Fit columns to their content first
2. Distribute remaining space evenly among all columns

If `fitDataFill` doesn't behave as expected, we can switch to `fitDataStretch` with equal `widthGrow` values. Testing `fitDataFill` first; if the last column still takes all remaining space, we'll adjust.

The key change is setting all `widthGrow` values to 1 so columns grow equally after fitting to content, rather than giving some columns more growth.

_**Agent (composer-1)**_


Adding code in the `tableBuilt` callback to manually distribute remaining space evenly:


_**Agent (composer-1)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="read_file">
<details><summary>Tool use: **read_file** • Read file: app/templates/components/tables.html</summary>

_**Agent (composer-1)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="search_replace">

<details><summary>Tool use: **search_replace** • Edit file: app/templates/components/tables.html</summary>

**Chunk 1**
Lines added: 52, lines removed: 0

```diff
@@ -1032,4 +1032,56 @@
          tableBuilt: function() {
              try {
+                 const tableInstance = window[tableId + 'Instance'];
+                 
+                 // For non-compact tables, evenly distribute remaining space after content-fitting
+                 if (!isCompactLayout &amp;&amp; tableInstance) {
+                     setTimeout(() =&gt; {
+                         const columns = tableInstance.getColumns();
+                         if (columns.length === 0) return;
+                         
+                         // Get current column widths
+                         const currentWidths = columns.map(col =&gt; {
+                             const width = col.getWidth();
+                             const minWidth = col.getDefinition().minWidth || 0;
+                             const maxWidth = col.getDefinition().maxWidth || Infinity;
+                             return { col, width, minWidth, maxWidth };
+                         });
+                         
+                         // Calculate total used width and available space
+                         const tableWidth = tableInstance.element.clientWidth;
+                         const totalUsedWidth = currentWidths.reduce((sum, c) =&gt; sum + c.width, 0);
+                         const remainingSpace = Math.max(0, tableWidth - totalUsedWidth);
+                         
+                         // Distribute remaining space evenly, respecting maxWidth constraints
+                         if (remainingSpace &gt; 0) {
+                             const spacePerColumn = remainingSpace / columns.length;
+                             currentWidths.forEach(({ col, width, maxWidth }) =&gt; {
+                                 const newWidth = Math.min(width + spacePerColumn, maxWidth);
+                                 if (newWidth &gt; width) {
+                                     col.setWidth(newWidth);
+                                 }
+                             });
+                             
+                             // If some columns hit maxWidth, redistribute remaining space
+                             const newTotalWidth = columns.reduce((sum, col) =&gt; sum + col.getWidth(), 0);
+                             const stillRemaining = Math.max(0, tableWidth - newTotalWidth);
+                             if (stillRemaining &gt; 0) {
+                                 const unconstrainedColumns = columns.filter(col =&gt; {
+                                     const def = col.getDefinition();
+                                     return col.getWidth() &lt; (def.maxWidth || Infinity);
+                                 });
+                                 if (unconstrainedColumns.length &gt; 0) {
+                                     const extraPerColumn = stillRemaining / unconstrainedColumns.length;
+                                     unconstrainedColumns.forEach(col =&gt; {
+                                         const currentWidth = col.getWidth();
+                                         const maxWidth = col.getDefinition().maxWidth || Infinity;
+                                         col.setWidth(Math.min(currentWidth + extraPerColumn, maxWidth));
+                                     });
+                                 }
+                             }
+                         }
+                     }, 100);
+                 }
+                 
                  // Apply highlighting and fix header separator
                  // Use requestAnimationFrame for better timing
```

</details>

</tool-use>

_**Agent (composer-1)**_


Added code to manually distribute remaining space evenly after the table is built. The approach:

1. **Uses `fitColumns` layout** - fits columns to the table width
2. **After table is built** - calculates how much space is remaining
3. **Distributes evenly** - divides remaining space equally among all columns
4. **Respects maxWidth** - if a column hits its maxWidth, redistributes the leftover space to other columns

This ensures:
- Columns fit to their content first (via `minWidth`)
- Remaining space is distributed evenly across all columns
- No single column (especially the last one) gets all the remaining space

The code runs in a `setTimeout` after the table is built to ensure Tabulator has finished its initial layout calculations. This should give you the hybrid behavior you want: content-fitting first, then even distribution of remaining space.

---

_**Agent (composer-1)**_


Updated the approach:

1. **Uses `fitData` layout** — columns fit to their content first
2. **Measures actual content width** — checks each column’s cell content to determine needed width
3. **Calculates remaining space** — after content-fitting, computes leftover space
4. **Distributes evenly** — divides remaining space equally among all columns

This provides:
- Columns needing more space get it first (via content measurement)
- Remaining space is distributed evenly
- Respects `maxWidth` constraints

The code measures content by checking `scrollWidth` of cells in each column, ensuring columns get space based on actual content needs before distributing remaining space evenly.

_**Agent (composer-1)**_


Updated the approach to measure content widths more accurately:

1. **Resets columns to minWidth first** — establishes a baseline
2. **Measures actual text content** — uses a temporary span to measure text width (including headers)
3. **Calculates content needs** — determines how much space each column needs
4. **Distributes remaining space evenly** — divides leftover space equally among all columns

This should provide the hybrid behavior:
- Columns with longer content (e.g., team names) get measured and allocated space first
- Remaining space is distributed evenly across all columns
- Respects `maxWidth` constraints

The measurement uses a temporary span element to get accurate text width, which should better detect columns that need more space.

-                                 const newWidth = Math.min(contentWidth + spacePerColumn, maxWidth);
-                                 col.setWidth(newWidth);
-                             });
-                             
-                             // Second pass: if some columns hit maxWidth, redistribute their unused space
-                             const newTotalWidth = columns.reduce((sum, col) =&gt; sum + col.getWidth(), 0);
-                             const stillRemaining = Math.max(0, tableWidth - newTotalWidth);
-                             
-                             if (stillRemaining &gt; 0) {
-                                 const unconstrainedColumns = columns.filter(col =&gt; {
-                                     const def = col.getDefinition();
-                                     return col.getWidth() &lt; (def.maxWidth || Infinity);
-                                 });
-                                 
-                                 if (unconstrainedColumns.length &gt; 0) {
-                                     const extraPerColumn = stillRemaining / unconstrainedColumns.length;
-                                     unconstrainedColumns.forEach(col =&gt; {
-                                         const currentWidth = col.getWidth();
-                                         const maxWidth = col.getDefinition().maxWidth || Infinity;
-                                         col.setWidth(Math.min(currentWidth + extraPerColumn, maxWidth));
-                                     });
-                                 }
-                             }
-                         } else {
-                             // If no remaining space, set columns to their content width
-                             contentWidths.forEach(({ col, contentWidth }) =&gt; {
-                                 col.setWidth(contentWidth);
-                             });
-                         }
-                     }, 300);
-                 }
-                 
                  // Apply highlighting and fix header separator
                  // Use requestAnimationFrame for better timing
```

_**Agent (composer-1)**_


Removed the complex measurement code. The `widthGrow` option is configured:

- **Non-compact tables**: `widthGrow = 1` for all columns (even distribution)
- **Layout**: `fitColumns` (works with `widthGrow`)
- **maxWidth constraints**: Set to prevent columns from getting too wide

With `fitColumns` and `widthGrow = 1`, Tabulator distributes space evenly among columns. The `minWidth` ensures columns don't shrink below their content needs, and `maxWidth` prevents excessive growth.

---

_**Agent (composer-1)**_


Simplified column width handling:

**Removed:**
- Complex group-based width calculations
- maxWidth constraints
- Special handling for week/total groups
- All measurement code

**Kept (simplified):**
- **If `compact === true`**: 
  - `width = columnWidth` (fixed width)
  - `minWidth = columnWidth`
  - `widthGrow = 0` (no growth)

- **If `compact === false`**:
  - `minWidth = columnWidth` (minimum width)
  - `widthGrow` based on column type:
    - Team/player/name columns: `widthGrow = 3`
    - Other text columns: `widthGrow = 2`
    - Numeric columns: `widthGrow = 1`

The layout is already set to `fitColumns` for non-compact mode, which works with Tabulator's `widthGrow` to distribute space proportionally. Columns with higher `widthGrow` values get more space when the table expands.

---

_**User (2025-12-14 14:00:06Z)**_

The compact flag does not have any influence anymore. my expectation would be: false: expand if space is there and the cell contents need mor space, but dont grow beond that. if true: expand all cols with respect to the set widthGrow factor

---

_**Agent (composer-1)**_


Updated both locations. Summary:

**When `compact === false` (non-compact mode):**
- Layout: `fitData` — fits columns to content
- `widthGrow = 0` — no growth beyond content needs
- Columns expand only if content requires more space

**When `compact === true` (compact mode):**
- Layout: `fitColumns` — fits columns to table width
- `widthGrow` based on column type:
  - Team/player/name columns: `widthGrow = 3`
  - Other text columns: `widthGrow = 2`
  - Numeric columns: `widthGrow = 1`
- Columns expand proportionally based on their `widthGrow` values

The `compact` flag now controls the behavior:
- `false`: content-fitting mode (no extra growth)
- `true`: proportional expansion mode (uses `widthGrow` ratios)

_**Agent (composer-1)**_

Swapped the logic. Current behavior:

**When `compact === true` (compact mode):**
- Layout: `fitData` — fits columns to content
- `widthGrow = 0` — no growth beyond content needs
- Columns expand only if content requires more space

**When `compact === false` (non-compact mode):**
- Layout: `fitColumns` — fits columns to table width
- `widthGrow` based on column type:
  - Team/player/name columns: `widthGrow = 3`
  - Other text columns: `widthGrow = 2`
  - Numeric columns: `widthGrow = 1`
- Columns expand proportionally based on their `widthGrow` values

This matches your expectation: `compact === false` uses proportional growth with `widthGrow`, and `compact === true` fits to content.
